When using the API in live mode the message 'Not logging full request/response headers and body in live mode for compliance' is constantly logged to the root logger at warning level. This is rather annoying as there's no way to suppress this log message since it is going to the root logger, short of setting the log level as higher than warning for the root logger (undesirable/bad practice).

The [section](https://github.com/paypal/PayPal-Python-SDK/blob/master/paypalrestsdk/api.py#L191) where the message is logged is the only bit in that file which uses `logging.debug` instead of `log.debug`, which would send it to the named logger for that file. Is that intentional, or simply an oversight?

In addition, even if those lines were changed to use `log.debug`, it seems like the message should be at the 'info' log level, not 'warning'? I don't want to disable all warnings for paypalrestsdk but that would be required to suppress the message in the logs.

I've created a pull request if these changes seem acceptable: #112

The directions and surface APIs both require `;` delimited `lon,lat` coordinate pairs. The directions API takes a json payload with a `coordinates` array. Future APIs may require different encodings for geometry inputs.

But python users are going to be dealing with different data structures: geojson-like objects, shapely geometries, fiona datasources, geodataframes, etc. All of these implement the `__geo_interface__` so that's a big win.

So **Question 0**: should we provide encoding functions or just leave it up to the user? *If* we assume the former, let's spec it out and define the behavior a bit more precisely.

**Question 1** Should the encoding happen external to the API wrapper or internally?
```
# external
waypoints_string = encode_points(features)
resp = directions.route(waypoints_string, ...)

# internal
direction.route(features, ...)
```

**Question 2** What can `features` consist of?
* An open fiona datasource? 
* A single feature collection
* An iterable of features
* An iterable of geojson geometries
* An iterable of shapely geometries
* A single geo object
* WKT? WKB?

I wrote a [vector data abstraction function](https://github.com/perrygeo/python-rasterstats/blob/master/src/rasterstats/io.py#L73) for rasterstats that solves a similar problem - pass it an object and it will try it's best to get an iterable of feature-like dicts out of it. Maybe a good model, maybe not depending on how liberal/strict we'd want to be with inputs.

**Question 3** Validation.
Should the encoding enforce known limitation on the API such as min/max number of points, geometry types (e.g. points only), precision requirements or others? Will geometries be "cast" i.e. multipoint encoded as a series of single points?

Just an idea at this point. But if we do it (and do it right) it could make interop with other python geo tools ridiculously smooth.
Would it be possible to make it available on Pip? Especially, I would need to install different versions per project depending on when I am working for Nao or Pepper.

Should investigate whether a shortened `.sel()` interface makes sense for NCSS (and RadarServer?) to do a one liner around `query()` and `get_data()`, possibly with a reduced set of support query terms.

issue_title: flake8 check does not respect projects python version
issue_body:
`pipenv check --style` appears to run the syntax check depending on its own python version instead of respecting the python version specified in the projects Pipfile.

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: Debian 9
1. Python version: `$ python3 -V`: `Python 3.5.3`
1. Pipenv version: `$ pipenv --version`: `pipenv, version 9.0.1`

No errors.

testfile.py

bar = 'baz'

Pipfile

issue_title: The difference between setup.py (pyproject.toml) and requirements.txt (Pipfile)
issue_body:
Why there even needs to be one? In PHP (Composer) and JS (npm, yarn), there is only one file type and libraries and applications work OK with these — libraries just don't need to commit their lockfile.

There is a difference between an application and a library, but on the pip's end, it's just a bunch of dependencies with versions specified.

issue_title: "--requires" flag is broken in all versions of Python.
issue_body:
When I run `python setupy.py --help` I see the following tantalizing option:

` --requires          print the list of packages/modules required`

But using it simply prints a newline to stdout. I've tried this on in 2.7/3.4 on my local Ubuntu install as well as on 2.7, 3.4, 3.5 and 3.6 CPython Docker images.

Expected behavior:
```
$ git clone https://github.com/pypa/sampleproject.git
$ cd sampleproject
$ python setup.py --requires
peppercorn
```

Observed behavior.

```
$ git clone https://github.com/pypa/sampleproject.git
$ cd sampleproject
$ python setup.py --requires

$ python3.4 setup.py --requires

```

`DefaultProvider._has` only checks that a file exists, and `DefaultProvider._get` reads it without catching permission issues, so if there's an unreadable file stuff will break.  The particular case I'm looking at is reading a PKG-INFO file on some scan on import, but I think it's a general issue.

One simple solution is to use `os.access` in `_has`?  In theory we should catch exceptions on the `open` call, but this isnt something that typically races so we can probably just do the simple thing.

issue_title: It's not a good idead to parse HTML text using regular expressions
issue_body:
In [`w3lib.html`](https://github.com/scrapy/w3lib/blob/master/w3lib/html.py) regular expressions are used to parse HTML texts:

```python
_ent_re = re.compile(r'&((?P<named>[a-z\d]+)|#(?P<dec>\d+)|#x(?P<hex>[a-f\d]+))(?P<semicolon>;?)', re.IGNORECASE)
_tag_re = re.compile(r'<[a-zA-Z\/!].*?>', re.DOTALL)
_baseurl_re = re.compile(six.u(r'<base\s[^>]*href\s*=\s*[\"\']\s*([^\"\'\s]+)\s*[\"\']'), re.I)
_meta_refresh_re = re.compile(six.u(r'<meta\s[^>]*http-equiv[^>]*refresh[^>]*content\s*=\s*(?P<quote>["\'])(?P<int>(\d*\.)?\d+)\s*;\s*url=\s*(?P<url>.*?)(?P=quote)'), re.DOTALL | re.IGNORECASE)
_cdata_re = re.compile(r'((?P<cdata_s><!\[CDATA\[)(?P<cdata_d>.*?)(?P<cdata_e>\]\]>))', re.DOTALL)
```

However this is definitely incorrect when it involves commented contents, e.g.

```python
>>> from w3lib import html
>>> html.get_base_url("""<!-- <base href="http://example.com/" /> -->""")
'http://example.com/'
```

Introducing "heavier" utilities like `lxml` would solve this issue easily, but that might be an awful idea as `w3lib` aims to be lightweight.  
Or maybe we could implement some quick parser merely for eliminating the commented parts.

Any ideas?

The idea is the move scrapy's `canonicalize_url()` helper to `w3lib.url`
Projects like frontera could be interested: see https://github.com/scrapinghub/frontera/pull/168#issuecomment-234558699

issue_title: Ideas for replacing "sparse run" with something more standard
issue_body:
With @dan-blanchard's work on a Python Topology DSL, he is able to reproduce all of the streamparse commands in pure Python with the exception of `sparse run`.

This is because `sparse run` has some Clojure interop code that creates a Storm `LocalCluster` in memory, and invokes it using a `StormTopology` instance that is made by the Clojure DSL.

One proposal for how to solve this issue is in issue #178, where we would add the ability to take a Python-defined Topology definition and serialize it as Thrift structs. We could use `thriftpy` to serialize the `StormTopology` instance to disk, and then use a small Java/Clojure command-line tool to load that Thrift definition from disk and pass it to a `LocalCluster` instance inside a JVM process. If we went for this approach, we'd still need `lein` since it would handle the actual dependency on the `LocalCluster` class from the Storm JAR. This might be OK since we're already relying upon `lein` for `.jar` file packaging.

Another approach is to take a closer look at Storm's new Flux contrib module. [As described here](https://github.com/apache/storm/tree/master/external/flux#yaml-configuration), Flux includes a YAML configuration, for which one option is to wire up a JVM class that returns a `StormTopology` instance. Combined with #178, the configured class could load the serialized `.thrift` file from disk. This could be nice since Flux is bundled with Storm and so we could instruct people to install Storm locally (e.g. in an `/opt` directory) and then rely upon Flux and an auto-generated Flux YAML configuration and perhaps a simple pre-compiled JAR that implements the Java class that Flux expects.

There may be other ways to solve this problem, for example there is some discussion of creating a subcommand that spins up a "real" local Storm topology in #179. Any other ideas from the community are welcome here.

I've been working on adapting the webterm example to work with our terminado replacement, and am encountering an issue when using it with xterm. Below is a Python example which adapts the webterm example to just run `vim` and display the output:

```python
import os
import pty
import shlex
import signal
import time

import pyte


class Terminal:
    def __init__(self, columns, lines, p_in):
        self.p_in = p_in
        self.screen = pyte.HistoryScreen(columns, lines)
        self.screen.set_mode(pyte.modes.LNM)
        self.screen.write_process_input = \
            lambda data: p_in.write(data.encode())
        self.stream = pyte.ByteStream()
        self.stream.attach(self.screen)

    def feed(self, data):
        self.stream.feed(data)

    def print(self):
        line_length = len(self.screen.display[0])
        print('+' + ('-' * line_length) + '+')
        for line in self.screen.display:
            print('|{}|'.format(line))
        print('+' + ('-' * line_length) + '+')


def open_terminal(command="bash", columns=40, lines=12):
    p_pid, master_fd = pty.fork()
    if p_pid == 0:  # Child.
        argv = shlex.split(command)
        env = dict(TERM="xterm", LC_ALL="en_GB.UTF-8",
                   COLUMNS=str(columns), LINES=str(lines))
        os.execvpe(argv[0], argv, env)

    # File-like object for I/O with the child process aka command.
    p_out = os.fdopen(master_fd, "w+b", 0)
    return Terminal(columns, lines, p_out), p_pid, p_out


if __name__ == "__main__":

    terminal, p_pid, p_out = open_terminal()

    time.sleep(0.1)
    p_out.write(b'vim\n')
    time.sleep(0.1)

    print('Before `write_process_input` has effect:')
    terminal.feed(p_out.read(65536))
    terminal.print()

    print('After `write_process_input` has effect:')
    terminal.feed(p_out.read(65536))
    terminal.print()

    os.kill(p_pid, signal.SIGTERM)
    p_out.close()
```

This generates the following output:

```
Before `write_process_input` has effect:
+----------------------------------------+
|                                        |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|                                        |
+----------------------------------------+
After `write_process_input` has effect:
+----------------------------------------+
|<9b>?6c                                 |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|~                                       |
|-- REPLACE --                           |
+----------------------------------------+
```

As you can see, some control characters get written into vim, causing it to enter replace mode and enter some text. These characters are written by pyte, which catches some control characters coming from the terminal output and calls `Screen.report_device_attributes`, which in turn sends `CSI + "?6c"` back into the terminal via the `write_process_input` method.

Comments in the code reference Linux's VT102 terminal emulation code, and indeed if I set `TERM='vt102'`, the problem goes away. Might it be the case that this code should only have an effect when the terminal process the screen is attached to is in a particular mode?

For the moment I am avoiding this issue by simply removing the attachment of `write_process_input` back onto the terminal process, but it would be good if anyone can provide feedback on whether this is the right thing to do.

Thanks,
Andrew

The current approach to adding and removing Plans to a Subscription is non-standard.  Our previous approach to things like this (eg. Create Cards [1] and Create Bank Account [2]) has been to use `*.create()` methods.  However, the approach for Plans in Multiplan Subscriptions is to override the attribute with a partial of the list and have that add to the full list on the backend and return something.

### Old Method
```py
# Retrieve the Subscription
sub = stripe.Subscription.retrieve("sub_xxx")

# Attach a New Subscription Item
sub.items = [{'plan': 'PLAN_ID1', 'quantity': 2}]

# Delete ~some Subscription Item (requires the ID)
sub.items = [{'id': 'si_xxx', 'deleted': True}]
```

### New Proposed Method
```py
# Retrieve Subscription
sub = stripe.Subscription.retrieve("sub_xxx")

# Attach a New Subscription Item
si_one = sub.items.create(plan="PLAN_ID1")

# Delete that Subscription Item
si_one.delete()
```

This affects other language bindings as well including PHP and Ruby.  May file those separately after this has been triaged.

@brandur-stripe believes that this should happen automatically, but it looks like that functionality is broken and we will need to investigate why.

[1] https://stripe.com/docs/api/python#create_card
[2] https://stripe.com/docs/api/python#customer_create_bank_account

## Description
I was trying to read the image at https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:JoeSimpson2012.JPG using `skimage.io.imread`. The [docs](http://scikit-image.org/docs/dev/api/skimage.io.html#skimage.io.imread) mention that the output will be an ndarray with the different colour bands/channels stored in the third dimension, such that a grey-image is MxN, an RGB-image MxNx3 and an RGBA-image MxNx4.

When doing this, I found that the output was a ndarray with 2 items. The first item has the ndarray mentioned in the docs, the second was an array whose repr gave: `array(<PIL.MpoImagePlugin.MpoImageFile image mode=RGB size=4320x3240 at 0xAC4FDF4C>, dtype=object)`.

## Way to reproduce
Here's a minimal example:
```python
#!/usr/bin/env python
# -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

from __future__ import (division, absolute_import, unicode_literals,
                        print_function)

import os
from shutil import copyfileobj

try:
    from urllib2 import urlopen
except ImportError:
    from urllib.request import urlopen

from skimage.io import imread

def download(url, filename, overwrite=False):
    if not os.path.exists(filename) or overwrite:
        response = urlopen(url)
        with open(filename, 'wb') as out_file:
            copyfileobj(response, out_file)

download('https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/e/ea/JoeSimpson2012.JPG',
         'JoeSimpson2012.JPG')

im = imread('JoeSimpson2012.JPG')
print('Shape of image data', im.shape)
print('Second element is an array with:', im[1])
print(im)
```

Note that `im[0]` is the output I was expecting. And it does work with other images I've used.

Versions:
 - scikit-image 0.12.3
 - python v2.7.11 (From Miniconda)
 - pip 8.1.1
 - numpy 1.11.0
 - Pillow 1.1.7

When applied to a labelled image with only one label `skimage.morphology.remove_small_objects` treats all of pixels with the one label as a single object. It does show a warning, that perhaps a boolean array was intended to be used, where the behaviour is different, where it treats all connected components separately.

First of all, the documentation is a bit vague about this - the description of the method just says it removes connected components, and only in the description of the `ar` variable it says "If the array type is int, it is assumed that it contains already-labeled objects" - which also doesn't directly say that the behaviour is then different.
http://scikit-image.org/docs/stable/api/skimage.morphology.html#skimage.morphology.remove_small_objects

Second of all, this inconsistency creates a problem during batch processing of many images, when after labelling you don't know how many objects you will actually have, and just a warning is not very helpful - for a long time I was just thinking it was related to efficiency (i.e. with a single label it's more efficient to use boolean arrays) and completely ignored it until I discovered it actually wasn't doing what I expected it to.

Therefore, I think, at the very least the documentation should be more explicit about this, and ideally the behaviour should be changed. I am not sure what would be the best solution here, perhaps adding a keyword to define which behaviour is desired? Or splitting this method into to, with different behaviours?

Thanks!

issue_title: Change API denoise_bilateral, multichannel=False by default.
issue_body:
## Description

See https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/pull/2461#issuecomment-273802520

in skimage/restoration/_denoise.py, deprecate mutlichannel=True to False for denoise_bilateral to be consistent with the rest of the functions. Arguments are listed in the link at the top of this message.

In doc/examples/filters/plot_denoise.py, the function call must be adapted as well.

## Description
[Please provide a general introduction to the issue/proposal.]
I believe 2 functions, transform.resize and rgb2gray, may have memory leaks.
[If reporting a bug, attach the entire traceback from Python.]
My  code:
def all_gray_all_same_size( images):
    # Rescale the images in the `images` array
    images28 = [transform.resize(image, (400, 400)) for image in images]

    images28 = np.array(images28)
    # Convert `images28` to grayscale
    images28 = rgb2gray(images28)
    return images28

It worked fine for a small data set. For a data set of 24000 jpg images (approx. 4 GB of data), it ended up using all 128 GB of RAM and crashing. I could watch the RAM usage going up and up and up over several minutes. The output was OOM (out of memory). I am running on a Linux supercomputer node with 28 processors and 128 GB of RAM.

Here's the relevant info:
Software
python/3.6 cuda/8.0.44

OS:
Linux 3.10.0-693.25.7.el7.x86_64
scikit image version: 0.13.0

issue_title: __init__.py import doesn't work correctly w/ Python 3.6
issue_body:
Heyo,

The line ```from sumologic import *``` in __init__.py (https://github.com/SumoLogic/sumologic-python-sdk/blob/2ca5e97e4f7514a98cdf8ddd372a9ff00ea8b8a1/sumologic/__init__.py) doesn't work correctly in Python 3.6 because it uses an absolute import, and not a relative import.  It ends up just importing its own (currently empty) package.  As a result, ```from sumologic import SumoLogic``` doesn't work, and you have to import ```sumologic.sumologic``` to access the class:

```
$ python
Python 3.6.0 (default, Dec 24 2016, 00:01:50)
[GCC 4.2.1 Compatible Apple LLVM 8.0.0 (clang-800.0.42.1)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import sumologic
>>> dir(sumologic)
['__builtins__', '__cached__', '__doc__', '__file__', '__loader__', '__name__', '__package__', '__path__', '__spec__', 'sumologic']
>>>
>>> from sumologic import SumoLogic
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
ImportError: cannot import name 'SumoLogic'
>>>
>>> from sumologic.sumologic import SumoLogic
>>> SumoLogic
<class 'sumologic.sumologic.SumoLogic'>
```

While calling method, which is designed to return no response (`servers.force_delete` for example) JSONDecodeError is being raised:
```
simplejson.errors.JSONDecodeError: Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)
Expecting value: line 1 column 1 (char 0)
```
This is caused by OpenStack sending header `Content-Type: application/json` in response, but without any body. This leads `simple-rest-client` to [loading json from empty string](https://github.com/allisson/python-simple-rest-client/blob/master/simple_rest_client/request.py#L28), which fails

### What did you do?

Execute these commands (Dockerfile) from a CentOS 7 base image:

```
RUN set -x \
  && pip install --upgrade pip \
  && pip install --upgrade setuptools \
  && pip install pexif==0.15 \
  && pip install -e git+https://github.com/thumbor/thumbor.git@master#egg=thumbor \
  && pip install opencv-engine==1.0.1 \
  && pip install thumbor-s3==0.2.0 \
  && pip install tc-prometheus==0.1.1 \
  && pip uninstall -y pillow || true \
  && pip install -U --force-reinstall pillow-simd \
  && python -c "from PIL import Image; print(Image.PILLOW_VERSION)" \
  && pip list
```

### What did you expect to happen?

That pillow-simd is a replacement package for pillow.

### What actually happened?

I get this this error message when running thumbor:

```
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/bin/thumbor", line 6, in <module>
    from pkg_resources import load_entry_point
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3142, in <module>
    @_call_aside
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3126, in _call_aside
    f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3155, in _initialize_master_working_set
    working_set = WorkingSet._build_master()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 664, in _build_master
    ws.require(__requires__)
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 981, in require
    needed = self.resolve(parse_requirements(requirements))
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 867, in resolve
    raise DistributionNotFound(req, requirers)
pkg_resources.DistributionNotFound: The 'Pillow<5.0.0,>=3.0.0' distribution was not found and is required by thumbor
```

### What versions of Pillow and Python are you using?

Pillow: 4.3.0.post0
Python: 2.7.5
Looks like we run the tests via `tox -v --recreate`. We should add a note in the docs about this, along with some info about `virualenv`, `setup.py` and `nose`.

issue_title: TM: The SegmentOverlap is making the code awkward
issue_body:
My [recent changes to the algorithm](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.core/pull/1059) have made the [activatePredictedColumn](https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/18ef587aba3654440dd8db20e87e2d96894e8b4c/src/nupic/research/temporal_memory.py#L320) function pretty hard to read.

The reason it's so weird is because "overlaps" are stored alongside segments in a `SegmentOverlap` struct. So if we want the number of active potential synapses for an active segment, we have to find its corresponding matching segment. That's messy.

I created the `SegmentOverlap`, but I've never really liked it. Another problem with it is that we're also storing the overlap for every active segment (i.e. the "number of active connected synapses"), but this is never getting consumed. We only use the `.overlap` on matching segments, not active segments. So this is unnecessary memory use.

It will be much better if the TM kept a list of `Segment` instances, and had a fast way to look up the "number of active potential synapses" for any segment.

(I have a C++ proof of concept working)

issue_title: Avoid need to write dumb getters/setters - a decorator, direct access,..?
issue_body:
So..I got tired of writing simple getters/setters in obvious cases, like:
```
self.x = 1
def setX(self, x):
  self.x=x
def getX(self):
  return self.x
```
Of course it has to be done for more complex cases with some computation, checks, etc. 

Read here: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/6618002/python-property-versus-getters-and-setters 
It seems the "pythonic way" would be to differentiate variables by underscore in name, w/o underscore means "public" and is a part of the API. The just use direct access for the variables.

That would be nice, but I see the problem in `c++/swig` seamless implementation - is there a way to fix this for that case? 

An option would be to create a decorator that creates a get/set function for that member. 

What do you think? 
@scottpurdy @rhyolight @oxtopus

issue_title: WebSubmit: improve access control for submissions "splash" page ("page 0")
issue_body:
Originally on 2012-07-11

Commit 7ab31e1db9f7d22b061c3e2b25f8dd9c0176f890 introduced increased access control of the submissions splash page, but also made it impossible for any user to proceed with the submission when there is no "category" (i.e. checkboxes) AND no displayed action (i.e. blue buttons) on the page. If possible the following snippet should be removed/improved:

```
@@ -1417,6 +1428,12 @@ def action(req, c=CFG_SITE_NAME, ln=CFG_SITE_LANG, doctype=""):
             actionbutton.append(action_details[4])
             statustext.append(action_details[5])

+    if not snameCateg and not actionShortDesc:
+        return page_not_authorized(req, "../submit",
+                                   uid=uid,
+                                   navmenuid='submit')
+
+
```

The implementation does not see the difference between no action+category displayed for user, and no action+category configured to be displayed for this submission. One possible improvement would be to not check against the result returned by websubmit_dblayer.get_actions_on_submission_page_for_doctype(...), but against websubmitadmin_dblayer.get_all_submissionnames_doctype(...).

(a sample use case at CERN could be GENSBM, but also it would more widely apply to any deprecated submission that would be left as a placeholder without any action+category, but only with some message informing about the deprecation and possible alternatives)

issue_title: bibtask signal handling should be improved
issue_body:
Originally on 2011-03-17

When bibtasks are put to sleep or stopped or killed, the corresponding signal should automatically be sent to any process that was indirectly launched by the task.

This is in particular is needed if the task don't specify a callback function for the stop signal.

An equivalent callback function should be allowed to be specified for the sleep signal.

Originally on 2014-01-20

It would be nice if BatchUploader would have some configurable limits, similar to MultiEdit (see also #77). In this way, any consequent processing jobs (indexing, etc.) would not impact the normal flow of the system.

Example:
  -   1 -> 500 records: add/update on the spot
  - 500 -> above: schedule the job for the evening

Currently our admin interfaces is constrained in space (e.g. tables gets squeeze and there's not much real estate), and design is not particularly nice.

2. Here are examples of free templates:

  - http://www.blacktie.co/2014/07/dashgum-free-dashboard/
  - http://www.egrappler.com/templatevamp-free-twitter-bootstrap-admin-template/
  - http://startbootstrap.com/template-overviews/sb-admin-2/

Alternatively we could build something ourselves and model it over something like:
http://demo.neontheme.com/dashboard/main-3/

issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: Normal metaclass resolution not applied to InterfaceClass
issue_body:
In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.interface/+bug/791218, @lrowe reported:

> While experimenting with my InterfaceClass subclass I noticed that it was only being used when it was specified as the first of the bases. I believe this is because InterfaceClass is not a subclass of `type`, so the normal metaclass derivation logic is not applied. The branch at svn+ssh://svn.zope.org/repos/main/zope.interface/branches/elro-metaclass-resolution implements that logic in `InterfaceClass.__new__`, picking from the base metaclasses that metaclass which subclasses all other base metaclasses.
> 
> The motivating factor here is: I need an Interface with a hook that gets called after `InterfaceClass.__init__` to allow for:
> - Checking that field names supplied as tagged values from directives correspond to actual field names to detect typos.
> - Registering the `Interface` instance for further configuration to be executed by a zope.configuration action.
> 
> Relevant changeset: http://zope3.pov.lt/trac/changeset/121497/zope.interface/branches/elro-metaclass-resolution
> 
> Example InterfaceClass subclass: http://dev.plone.org/plone/browser/plone.supermodel/branches/elro-directives/plone/supermodel/model.py#L58

In https://bugs.launchpad.net/zope.exceptions/+bug/98389, @mgedmin reported:

> I used `zope.exceptions.exceptionformatter.format_exception(as_html=False)` in an application to show tracebacks in views. I have encountered situations when `format_exception` returned me a list of UTF-8 strings (my system locale is UTF-8), other situations where I got a list of Unicode strings, and even situations where the list contained a mixture of UTF-8 and Unicode strings (causing UnicodeDecodeErrors in `''.join(...)`).
> 
> The contract of the exception formatter should explicitly say whether it returns Unicode or not, and various formatting methods should take care to convert the strings as appropriate. I would prefer to get Unicode always.
> 
> Source of 8-bit characters: `linecache.getline`, `traceback.format_exception_only`. Suspected source of Unicode strings: `__traceback_info__`/`__traceback_supplement__`.
> 
> There are some calls to `str()` and, `log.Formatter` uses `cStringIO` -- these would break with Unicode strings.
> 
> (Side issue: `TextExceptionFormatter` also sometimes performs HTML escaping.)

issue_title: Idea - Import and export support for DataPackages
issue_body:
[DataPackages](http://data.okfn.org/doc/data-package) is a project that offers a standardized and extensible way of packaging up data. There is already a growing community around Data Packages and an increasing number of core datasets which can easily be grabbed from a URL.

It would be great to see easy import and export to and from a Tabular Data Package - see spec here - into a Pandas DataFrame (e.g. pd.from_datapackage(...) & pd.to_datapackage(...)).

Moved from main Pandas repot to here on advice of @TomAugspurger
In `QtCore.py` a few variables are renamed but the old names still persist, like `pyqtSignal` and `Signal`. PyQt versions should be removed.

I want to do something like:

```
try:
    import QtPy
except ImportError:
    pass
```

and have this also catch the case where PyQt5 or PySide aren't installed. However, in those cases the exception is ``PythonQtError`` rather than ``ImportError``. However, I can't catch ``PythonQtError`` because I can't do ``from qtpy import PythonQtError`` since QtPy won't import...

Since I don't watch to just catch ``Exception``, could ``PythonQtError`` be made a subclass of ``ImportError``?

When attempting to create a Saved Query by passing in a dict representing the query attributes, the SDK fails unless one passes that dict as a json-formatted str. This differs from other parts of the SDK like ```add_event()```. The solution is to do as ```KeenApi.post_events()``` does and call ```json.dumps()``` to format the dict properly for a PUT body before passing it as the ```data``` argument to ```fulfill()```: https://github.com/keenlabs/KeenClient-Python/blob/master/keen/saved_queries.py#L68

import keen
from keen.client import KeenClient

keen.project_id = '<id>'
keen.master_key = '<key>'
client = KeenClient(project_id=keen.project_id, master_key=keen.master_key)

new_saved_count_query = {

        'analysis_type': 'average',
        'target_property': 'price',
        'event_collection': 'purchases',
        'timeframe': 'this_2_weeks'

}

result',

```

...

    keen_api._error_handling(response)
  File "C:\Python27\lib\site-packages\keen\api.py", line 219, in _error_handling

Hi again @rmjarvis - another config question for you:

I have a case where I might want to make two versions of the same simulation: one noise-free and one with added noise.  I was looking at the output options on [here](https://github.com/GalSim-developers/GalSim/wiki/Config-Output) to see if there is an obvious way to do this - e.g., to put the noise-free image in one HDU and the noisy one in another.  But that doesn't seem to be possible.  I would guess I could make a custom output type, but I don't think this is a totally crazy thing to want to do, so I was wondering if it would be possible (maybe even easy?) to add this as a config option? We should do that too!

- One line description of what the demo is about
- Key code features that are introduced in the demo
- Links to the .py and .yaml files

issue_title: Capture Definition Provenance from other files and class definitions
issue_body:
Add an option to collect definition provenance from other modules other than the main script. 

Currently, we capture only the content hash of the main script as definition provenance. The other local modules are captured as deployment provenance. So, we do not capture definition provenance from them that are useful for execution provenance (such as global variables names)

We could expand FunctionDef table to not only store function definitions, but also classes and files, since they have the same attributes: trial_id, id, name, code_hash.
We could also add an extra attribute: parent.
This way, the following code would generate 4 definition objects
```python
# some_file.py
class SomeClass(object):
    def some_method(self):
        def some_function():
            pass
```

An extra attribute, qualname, could be provided for qualified name reference, according to https://www.python.org/dev/peps/pep-3155/ 

Definition objects:
```
Definition(id=1, name="some_file.py", parent=None, qualname="")
Definition(id=2, name="SomeClass", parent=1, qualname="SomeClass")
Definition(id=3, name="some_method", parent=2, qualname="SomeClass.some_method")
Definition(id=4, name="some_function", parent=3, qualname="SomeClass.some_method.<local>.some_function")
```

Note that the qualname is not fully qualified, so we don't have the filename on it.

%build

%install

issue_title: Future upgrades - always replicas then leader?
issue_body:
From v1.0 release notes:

> When upgrading from v0.90 or below, always upgrade all replicas before the master. Since we don't store replication credentials in DCS anymore, an old replica won't be able to connect to the new master.

Is this requirement just for upgrading to v1.0 because of change in where replicas get credentials for leader? Or will this "replicas first, leader last" requirement be for all future updates too?

I ask because we run a fleet of patroni clusters randomly scheduled in docker containers across a fleet of machines. We've not coded a deterministic upgrade sequence - rather containers on a specific machine (some leaders and some replicas) will be upgraded, then it moves on to upgrade other machines and their containers. Our workaround for the v1.0 upgrade might be to do disaster recovery - destroy everything, and rebuild all clusters from wal-e backups. So, currently am wondering if this issue will reoccur for future patroni v1.X releases?

This feature request is for the gtk3-based branch.

`VTE` 0.49.1 (and `GNOME Terminal` 3.25.1) has just implemented a brand new and hopefully really cool feature: hyperlinks (that is, HTML-like anchors).

This is different from the regex-based autodetection of URLs that appear onscreen. This time the text that appears does not have to look like a URL, it can be anything, and the target URL is specified explicitly by the utility along with the displayed text, using a particular escape sequence.

In case you'd like to hook up to this new feature (I hope you do), here's what to do:

- Call `vte_terminal_set_allow_hyperlink()` to enable the feature, as it's disabled by default.

- Whenever you're interested in the target URL at a certain location (e.g. for Ctrl+click action, or for presenting the right-click menu), call `vte_terminal_hyperlink_check_event()`. This returns a newly allocated string containing the URI-encoded URL that you should eventually free.

- If you'd like to stay up to date with the URL as the mouse moves or the contents underneath change, hook up to the `hyperlink-hover-uri-changed` signal. It'll give you two parameters owned by VTE (that is, must not be modified or freed, and only valid for the duration of the signal handler). The first is the URI-encoded URL, the second is a bounding rectangle of the anchor text (relative to GtkWindow's topleft). [GNOME Terminal shows a tooltip with the URI-decoded and un-IDN'd URL (this decoding is done by GNOME Terminal, not by VTE). It's up to you to choose different presentations if you wish, e.g. an overlay in the bottom corner similarly to browsers, or whatever you prefer. There's even a pending demo patch for GNOME Terminal to show a preview for certain local files (most notably pictures, PDFs).]

When handling `file:` URLs, please double check that you verify the hostname, as described in [1]. This is unfortunately not done by `gtk_show_uri()` or its underlying `g_app_info_launch_default_for_uri()`.

In case a character cell belongs to both an explicit URL (this new feature) and an implicit autodetected one (the old one, using regex match), we recommend the explicit one to take precedence (we assume that the application emitting the URL knows it much better than the autodetecting regex). This is also how VTE underlines the cells, so this is what the user expects on e.g. a Ctrl+click. The right-click menu might display both entries, in GNOME Terminal we decided to display actions for the explicit hyperlink only in case of such collision.

Keep in mind that we're in a development cycle, there's a slim chance that the API will change before it's finalized in VTE 0.50 this September. I'll let you know here if it changes.

For further information on this feature, see

[1] A dedicated page with all the details: https://gist.github.com/egmontkob/eb114294efbcd5adb1944c9f3cb5feda

[2] The GNOME Terminal bugreport (way too long and probably not that interesting at this point): https://bugzilla.gnome.org/show_bug.cgi?id=779734

**models.py**
```python
class Account(models.Model):
    username = models.CharField(max_length=40)
    service = models.CharField(max_length=40)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.username

    class Meta:
        verbose_name = 'Account'
        verbose_name_plural = 'Accounts'


class Contact(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=40)
    email = models.EmailField(max_length=60)

issue_title: FourDD algorithm segmentation faults with >999 sounding heights
issue_body:

This limitation is mentioned in the documentation of the [`dealias_fourdd` function](http://arm-doe.github.io/pyart-docs-travis/user_reference/generated/pyart.correct.dealias_fourdd.html) but it would be better if a this behavior raised an error with a more expressive message.

``` Python
import numpy as np
import pyart

radar = pyart.testing.make_empty_ppi_radar(10, 360, 2)
radar.range['meters_between_gates'] = 10
radar.range['meters_to_center_of_first_gate'] = 10
radar.instrument_parameters = {'nyquist_velocity': {'data': np.ones((720, ))}}
radar.add_field('velocity', {'data': np.zeros((720, 10))})

height = np.arange(2000)
speed = np.zeros((2000, ))
direction = np.zeros((2000, ))

unfoldedvel = pyart.correct.dealias_fourdd(
    radar, sounding_heights=height, sounding_wind_speeds=speed,
    sounding_wind_direction=direction, gatefilter=False)
```

issue_title: Test experimental workflow with public data
roundf is not included in math.h for Visual Studio 2010
issue_body:
although part of C99 standard, the `roundf` function was only included in MS Visual Studio only from version 2013:
http://blogs.msdn.com/b/vcblog/archive/2013/07/19/c99-library-support-in-visual-studio-2013.aspx

When I try to build the AFKDO using Visual Studio 2010, I get the following error:
```
"C:\UsersLocal\cosimo.lupo\Documents\GitHub\afdko\FDK\Tools\Programs\tx\build\win\vc10\tx.sln" (Build target) (1) -> "C:\UsersLocal\cosimo.lupo\Documents\GitHub\afdko\FDK\Tools\Programs\tx\build\win\vc10\tx.vcxproj" (default target)
(2) ->
(Link target) ->
  absfont.lib(absfont_dump.obj) : error LNK2019: unresolved external symbol _roundf referenced in function _writeRea l [C:\UsersLocal\cosimo.lupo\Documents\GitHub\afdko\FDK\Tools\Programs\tx\build\win\vc10\tx.vcxproj]
  svgwrite.lib(svgwrite.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol _roundf [C:\UsersLocal\cosimo.lupo\Document s\GitHub\afdko\FDK\Tools\Programs\tx\build\win\vc10\tx.vcxproj]
  ufowrite.lib(ufowrite.obj) : error LNK2001: unresolved external symbol _roundf [C:\UsersLocal\cosimo.lupo\Document s\GitHub\afdko\FDK\Tools\Programs\tx\build\win\vc10\tx.vcxproj]
  ..\..\..\exe\win\release\tx.exe : fatal error LNK1120: 1 unresolved externals [C:\UsersLocal\cosimo.lupo\Documents \GitHub\afdko\FDK\Tools\Programs\tx\build\win\vc10\tx.vcxproj]
```

There are three modules where the roundf is used, all in the lib: `absfont_dump.c`, `svgwrite.c` and `ufowrite.c`. Because of the unresolved external symbol, the tools `tx`, `mergeFonts` and `rotateFont` cannot be successfully built on Windows using vc10.
The *.sln files version read "Visual Studio 2010", and also the name of the folders for the Windows build is "vc10" (i.e. MS Visual Studio 2010). So one would expect to compile the AFDKO code with Visual Studio 2010.

If I define the `roundf` function like below, then I can successfully compile everything:

```c
/* roundf was only included in math.h as of MS Visual Studio 2013, so we must define our own. */
#if _MSC_VER < 1800
static float roundf(float num)
{
	float integer = ceilf(num);
	if (num > 0)
		return integer - num > 0.5f ? integer - 1.0f : integer;
	return integer - num >= 0.5f ? integer - 1.0f : integer;
}
#endif
```

(I found the code for roundf [here](http://www.opensource.apple.com/source/JavaScriptCore/JavaScriptCore-721.26/wtf/MathExtras.h), but there's nothing special about it).

However, the organization of the AFDKO sources is very complex and I don't know where to place this function so that I can include it in all the required modules.

Could anybody help me out?

Hi,

I have to connect to a mail server (Exchange) where emails are used as usernames. At the moment `HttpNtlmAuth` constructor finds `@` in my username [here](https://github.com/requests/requests-ntlm/blob/master/requests_ntlm/requests_ntlm.py#L31) and splits it into a domain and username pair.

This was added in @nitzmahone's pull request https://github.com/requests/requests-ntlm/pull/68.

In this particular case this is incorrect as domain should be empty. But it may also be incorrect in the general case. According to [this](http://davenport.sourceforge.net/ntlm.html#nameVariations), domain should always be empty when username is in kerberos-style format.

It is easy to work around this as I can change domain after `HttpNtlmAuth` was constructed. But should we add optional `domain` parameter to the constructor and/or not split usernames?

Regards,
Srdan

```
λ pip freeze | grep ntlm
ntlm-auth==1.0.2
requests-ntlm==1.0.0
```

issue_title: Support both seconds from epoch and timestamp string
issue_body:
If someone already has a timestamp string, we shouldn't force them to convert it to `datetime.datetime` so that we can in turn [convert that][2] into seconds. (Inspired by #1381.)

From [the docs][1]

> TIMESTAMP data types can be described in two ways: UNIX timestamps or calendar datetimes. BigQuery stores TIMESTAMP data internally as a UNIX timestamp with microsecond precision.

so we could allow the user to just pass a string and we "take their word for it" that it's a valid datetime. Since `Table.insert_data` returns a list of errors anyhow, we wouldn't have to do much/anything to enforce this.

[1]: https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/preparing-data-for-bigquery#datatype
[2]: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/gcloud-python/blob/0c74a599b6aa0a16e21575998e1770182c05e12c/gcloud/bigquery/table.py#L665-L669

@GoogleCloudPlatform/devrel-samples FYI

I stumbled upon this while deploying my app for the first time on GKE/GCE.

I had to explicitly specify `project` when creating a client: `gcloud.datastore.Client(project="my-project")`, otherwise I just got an `gcloud.exceptions.ServiceUnavailable: 503 Backend Error`.

First I thought the problem would be that autodetecting the project-id didn't work, but `gcloud.datastore.Client(credentials=credentials, project=project)` returns my project-id, be it as `bytes` instead of `str`.

I quickly verified that using `bytes` as a project-id does not work, i.e. `gcloud.datastore.Client(project=b"my-project")` resulted in that neat `503 Backend Error`.

issue_title: Pickling a Sympy lambdified expression causes built-in functions to be replaced with Numpy functions
issue_body:
I found some very strange behavior where using dill.dump to store a Sympy lambdified expression causes built-in functions such as min, max, abs, etc. to be replaced with Numpy functions of the same name. 

```
from sympy import symbols, lambdify
import dill, inspect

def check_if_builtin(func):
    try:
        file = inspect.getsourcefile(func) #will throw TypeError for builtin
        return file
    except TypeError:
        return True



dill.settings['recurse'] = True #without this option, throws PicklingError

a, b, c = symbols("a b c")
expr = a + b + c
lambda_expr = lambdify([a, b, c], expr)

print(check_if_builtin(min))

dill.dump(lambda_expr, open('test.p', 'wb'))

print(check_if_builtin(min))
```

returns:

```
True
C:\Anaconda3\lib\site-packages\numpy\core\fromnumeric.py
```

Naturally this breaks my code and I'm currently using a hackish fix to replace the builtins after pickling. Here is my setup:

Python 3.5.1
Windows 10 64-bit
dill: 0.2.5
sympy: 0.7.6.1
numpy: 1.11.0

issue_title: glyph-icons are showing up as rectangles
Windows encoding of __init__.py breaks setup with certain locales
issue_body:
The ```setup.py``` script parses the version number from the ```adal/__init__.py```. This can fail with certain locales on Linux when Python3 is being used:

```bash
glaubitz@suse-laptop:~/upstream/azure-activedirectory-library-for-python> LANG=C python3 setup.py build
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "setup.py", line 35, in <module>
    open('adal/__init__.py').read()).group(1)
  File "/usr/lib64/python3.6/encodings/ascii.py", line 26, in decode
    return codecs.ascii_decode(input, self.errors)[0]
UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xef in position 0: ordinal not in range(128)
glaubitz@suse-laptop:~/upstream/azure-activedirectory-library-for-python>
```

This is because of the Windows encoding of ```__init__.py``` contains a byte-order mark at the beginning of the file:

```
<U+FEFF>#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
```

simply converting ```__init__.py``` to standard Unix format fixes the problem:

```bash
glaubitz@suse-laptop:~/upstream/azure-activedirectory-library-for-python> dos2unix adal/__init__.py 
dos2unix: converting file adal/__init__.py to Unix format...

issue_title: 折腾了好久，elixir版的一个实现终于可以稳定运行了，喜欢折腾的可以搞搞
issue_body:
经过一段时间的折腾，首先搞了一个ruby版的实现，虽然可以用，但感觉除了能用来研究工作原理，屁用都没有，真心感觉跑起来太他妈慢了，严重吃内存，用具俗话来描述就是干啥啥不行，吃啥啥没够，于是果断放弃ruby实现，该用elixir按照同样的思路重新出发，经过边学习边研究，在踩了无数个坑，马上就要摔死的时候，终于见亮了，服务可以稳定运行了（至少在过去的48小时之内，服务一直在稳定运行着，使用人数在10人左右）。

我只对AES-256-CFB做了实现，其它的以后再慢慢加。在这个实现中，我加入了数据库支持，用户信息可以通过 `文件` 或 `数据库` 两种方式来管理，而且还实现了简单的命令来添加/修改用户（删除用户还没有搞），通过命令添加／修改用户之后无需重启服务，就会自动生效（其实就是轮询，得到更新的记录，然后重新启动相关子进程），另外存到数据库里的密码是经过加密的，所以安全性方面应该有所提高。文件方式保存的用户信息，密码还是明文的，这个跟python版的实现一样，不过我用yaml替换了json。

有兴趣的同学可以看一下这个实现，最初的实现版本，150+代码，也是可以稳定运行的版本：
https://github.com/ssselixir/ssselixir/tree/single_user

项目地址在这里：
https://github.com/ssselixir/ssselixir

note that the PYTHONPATH does not contain the `pex1` path - but manually appending this to sys.path causes the import to work.

the existing `PEX_PATH` handling seems to be specific to 3rdparty deps as handled here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/blob/master/pex/pex.py#L70-L79

but the requisite sys.path manipulation required to import sources from the `PEX_PATH`'d pex itself seems to live in the boilerplate here: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/blob/master/pex/pex_builder.py#L39-L40

so it seems like we'd probably want to extract a PEX._process_pex_paths() method that would provide a list of additional envs and sys.path components; then make PEX._activate() take an `extra_envs` param and PEX.patch_sys() take an `extra_paths` param; then in PEX.execute() call _process_pex_paths() early on and plumb the extras to _activate() and _patch_sys(). or something along those lines.

We should add a `--[no]-transitive` flag for all lint tasks to support the usecase where you're running directly on changed targets.

Running directly on changed targets happens when you use `./pants --changed lint` (for any setting of the `--changed-include-dependees` option): in that case, you know that only files directly owned by a target are affected. It also happens during `arc lint`.

Because this affects so many tasks, it seems likely that it should go on a (perhaps lint specific?) Subsystem and be consumed there.

issue_title: Python Thrift caching issue with included Thrift files
issue_body:
Test case available on the `thrift-include` branch of https://github.com/wyattanderson/pantstest/tree/thrift-include

### Problem
Under-invalidation when modifying transitive Thrift dependencies of a Thrift target. We have a Thrift API in `A.thrift` that imports `B.thrift` and `C.thrift`. `B` and `C` are consumable via separate targets as well. `A` depends on `B` and `C` in the BUILD file in addition to importing them:

```
python_thrift_library(
    name='A',
    sources=[
        'A.thrift',
    ],
    dependencies=[
        ':B',
        ':C',
    ]
)

python_thrift_library(
    name='B',
    sources=[
        'B.thrift',
    ]
)

python_thrift_library(
    name='C',
    sources=[
        'C.thrift',
    ]
)
```

The most reliable reproducer I can find is **in a project with artifact caching enabled, make a change to `C.thrift` and run/build a Python target that depends on `A.thrift`. The changes won't be picked up unless you `clean-all`**.

### Setup
1. Clone the repository
1. `./pants binary src/python:test && dist/test.pex`
1. Expected output: 

        ['C', 'read', 'thrift_spec', 'validate', 'write']
1. Uncomment lines 5-7 in `src/thrift/C.thrift`
1. `./pants binary src/python:test && dist/test.pex`

### Expected output 
    ['C', 'Cba', 'Cfoo', 'Cfoobar', 'read', 'thrift_spec', 'validate', 'write']

### Actual output
    ['C', 'read', 'thrift_spec', 'validate', 'write']

If you run `./pants clean-all`, you get the expected output, which makes me think this is an artifact caching issue. The actual problem we're seeing in production is even stranger: the addition of a new type to the equivalent of the `C.thrift` file doesn't get included in the built PEX.

issue_title: cache fetching hang: subproc_map result still not ready
issue_body:
Running off Pants sha af9ed0cf8f17e513759a357f98ee734ba76f535f some users are seeing fetching from the cache hang. They don't seem to have any packet loss or similar network issues.

[cache]
                       JavaCompile will read from local artifact cache at ...
                       JavaCompile will read from remote artifact cache at ...
                       subproc_map result still not ready...
                       subproc_map result still not ready...
                       subproc_map result still not ready...

Where does the logging for each of the subprocesses go?

I'd like to be able to ask pants to go "offline" entirely. This would stop any efforts to make HTTP or other network requests (instead complaining that it's offline), and would need to be paired with a "go offline" command that predownloads all sorts of things that might be necessary to run offline. An example of such a use case would be working on a plane or in a secure environment that intentionally doesn't allow network access. I see a ton of options on http://www.pantsbuild.org/options_reference.html which would allow me to prevent individual tools (like e.g., ivy) from talking to the internet (by specifying a bad proxy, for example) but haven't seen anything obvious to ask the entire pants ecosystem to stay offline. Am I missing something?

issue_title: Add a config option to enable or disable parts of stats collecting
issue_body: Related to https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/pull/4693 where stats collection of JUnit test results caused slow tests from large XML files.  It would be nice to be able to enable or disable parts of the stats collection process.

There are a few different cases in which it is not useful (or safe) to store or lookup the Step for a Node in the cache.

A potential implementation of this would be to add a predicate (or list of predicates) to Engine that takes a `Step` and decides whether it is cacheable. Another potential implementation would be to add a similar method to Node that queries whether a particular instance is cacheable.

I'm trying to give a Lambda access to my DynamoDB table, and am running into an error about not having permissions on the GSI. Wouldn't it make more sense to include GSIs on the `DynamoDBCrudPolicy` rather than requiring someone to make a new policy, just to add access to a GSI?

AFAICT it's as easy as changing:
             `"arn:${AWS::Partition}:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${tableName}"`

To:

`"arn:${AWS::Partition}:dynamodb:${AWS::Region}:${AWS::AccountId}:table/${tableName}/*"`

And, moreso, from a semantics standpoint, if a Table has a GSI attached, wouldn't you assume that `DynamoDBCrudPolicy` would include it?

issue_title: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan cannot use a stage from  AWS::Serverless::Api
issue_body:
I have a AWS::Serverless::Api defined as 
```  MyApiResource:
     Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
     Properties:
        StageName: test
        DefinitionUri: swagger.json
```

and a AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan defined as 
```  UsagePlan:
    Type: AWS::ApiGateway::UsagePlan
    Properties:
      ApiStages:
        -
          ApiId: !Ref MyApiResource
          Stage: test
      Throttle:
        BurstLimit: 500
        RateLimit: 50
      UsagePlanName:
        Fn::Join: [
            "_",
            [
              "UsagePlan",
              !Ref "AWS::Region",
              !Ref "AWS::StackName"
            ]
          ]
```

If I run the cloud formation script it fails to create the UsagePlan with an error saying the stage test does not exist on the API.

If I instead comment out the usage plan and run the script and then run it again with the API and the usage plan enabled it will create the usage plan and link it to the stage properly.

Hello, I am using CodePipeline to build and deploy a lambda function (scaffolded out with codestar). I have a template.yml file where I define some parameters like this:
```
Parameters:
  ProjectId:
    Type: String
    Description: AWS CodeStar projectID used to associate new resources to team members
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Description: The environment in which the lambda function is exectuting (ALPHA, PROD, ...)
```

I would like to be able to add this one for EncryptionEnabled which can be true or false.


```
Parameters:
  ProjectId:
    Type: String
    Description: AWS CodeStar projectID used to associate new resources to team members
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Description: The environment in which the lambda function is exectuting (ALPHA, PROD, ...)
  EncryptionEnabled:
    Type: Boolean
    Description: a boolean value indicating whether request and response are encrypted.
```

However, in the deploy step of my codepipeline I get the error, 

> Action Execution Failed: Parameter [EncryptionEnabled] is invalid

I also tried boolean with a lowercase b but same error. Are boolean values not supported here? Am I just using the wrong value for the parameter's Type?

thanks.

```

issue_title: Mappings in template do not maintain quotes during package step
issue_body:
Hello, using `aws cloudformation package` with the following simple snippet results in the loss of quotes which causes a ChangeSet to remain in CREATE_IN_PROGRESS indefinitely in the proper scenerio.

``` yml
Mappings:
  '1234567890':
    kmskeys:
      'dev': 'arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:1234567890:key/1234567890-abcdefg-1234-1234'
      'test': 'arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:1234567890:key/1234567890-abcdefg-1234-12345'
```
after the `aws cloudformation package` step:
``` yml
Mappings:
  1234567890:
    kmskeys:
      dev: arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:1234567890:key/1234567890-abcdefg-1234-1234
      test: arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:1234567890:key/1234567890-abcdefg-1234-12345
```
This is a problem because when using the `!Ref "AWS::AccountID"` function, a string is returned so it will never match the mapping - if you are mapping per AccountID.

Per the [docs](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/using-cfn-updating-stacks-get-template.html), if a Mapping is not used, it is an invalid changeset

So, I'm curious if this is a bug. I feel like I should be able to create a Mapping with Account ID's and then reference the AccountID later in a later block and it is currently not possible with `aws cloudformation package`. Thoughts?

```
$ aws --version
aws-cli/1.11.47 Python/2.7.10 Darwin/16.0.0 botocore/1.5.10
````

This page refers to hook function examples in the examples folder, but as far as I can see there are none. The AWS docs don't seem to help either.

https://github.com/awslabs/serverless-application-model/blob/master/docs/safe_lambda_deployments.rst

issue_title: SetContentFile does not flush write buffer for file used before uploading
issue_body: as per title - hence you can get invalid files uploaded because the end few mb's are never uploaded

issue_title: Can we use proxy to access google drive ?
issue_body:

issue_title: json schema validation doesn't appear to be validating
issue_body:
When defining layer options according to JSON Schema, such as the following [link describes](http://spacetelescope.github.io/understanding-json-schema/reference/generic.html): i would expect any inheriting layer, that defines an option that doesn't exist in the list to error during  `charm build`

Given in the base layer:

```yaml
defines:
  storage-driver:
    enum: [overlay, aufs, btrfs, zfs]
    default: aufs
    description: |
      Used in conjunction with the storage volume in metadata.yaml.
      Supported providers, when prompted, will allow you to mount a block
      device, formatted for use with the storage driver configured here.
```

And in the inheriting layer:

```yaml
options:
  storage-driver:
    value: schenanigans
```

I expected during the build time, the base schema to be validated. ENUM type should have limited the values to those defined as valid: 'aufs', 'btrfs', 'zfs', or 'overlay' - anything outside of this pre-defined list when provided as a value should have actively prevented the build, or failed it, as it doesn't validate against the defined schema.

issue_title: flowspec: AND bit in the first operator is set
issue_body:
##### ISSUE TYPE

Bug Report

##### OS

ExaBGP is running on Ubuntu 16.04 Docker container on Ubuntu 16.04 host.

##### VERSION

```
# exabgp --version
ExaBGP : 4.0.2-1c737d99
Python : 3.5.2 (default, Nov 23 2017, 16:37:01)  [GCC 5.4.0 20160609]
Uname  : Linux 46616c3d145e 4.4.0-109-generic #132-Ubuntu SMP Tue Jan 9 19:52:39 UTC 2018 x86_64
Root   : /usr/local
```

##### ENVIRONMENT 

```
<EMPTY>
```

##### CONFIGURATION

```
neighbor 172.17.0.2 {
    router-id 192.168.0.2;
    local-address 172.17.0.3;
    local-as 65000;
    peer-as 65000;
}
```

##### SUMMARY

With the combined numeric type (maybe also bitmask type) rules like `port >=80&<=90`, ExaBGP seems to set the AND bit of in the first operator byte.
Wireshark shows `&& >=80 && <=90`;

```
...(snip)...
Border Gateway Protocol - UPDATE Message
    ...(snip)...
    Path attributes
        ...(snip)...
        Path Attribute - MP_REACH_NLRI
            ...(snip)...
            Network layer reachability information (6 bytes)
                FLOW_SPEC_NLRI (6 bytes)
                    ...(snip)...
                    Filter: Port filter (&& >=80 && <=90)
                    ...(snip)...
```

According to [RFC5575](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5575), it should be unset, then the above rule should be `>=80 && <=90` on Wireshark.

```
         a -   AND bit.  If unset, the previous term is logically ORed
               with the current one.  If set, the operation is a logical
               AND.  It should be unset in the first operator byte of a
               sequence.  The AND operator has higher priority than OR
               for the purposes of evaluating logical expressions.
```

##### STEPS TO REPRODUCE

After connection established, announce a route via "exabgpcli".

```
exabgpcli 'announce flow route { match { port >=80&<=90; } then { discard; } }'
```

I obviously couldn't get NetworkID with [containers](http://docker-py.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api/#containers) api. Other properties are good.

```
{
	'Networks': {
		'myNetWork': {
			'IPAddress': '172.30.0.17',
			'IPPrefixLen': 16,
			...
			'NetworkID': '',             <========
			...
		}
	}
}
```

Is this problem or am I wrong?

> Docker version 1.11.1,
docker-py 1.8.1,
pip 8.1.2

Here is a test script.

```py
#!/usr/bin/python3

from docker import Client
cl = Client(base_url="unix://var/run/docker.sock")

# get containers
cons = cl.containers()

for con in cons:
        print(con["Names"])
        print(con["Image"])
        print(con["NetworkSettings"])
        break
```

Right now `samples` key is an alias for `solutions` list. `solutions` is returned by SAPI, and we introduced `samples` as it sometimes makes more sense from user's perspective.

Now that we're implementing pre/post-processing of parameters/response (see #199, #200), it makes sense to have `samples` be a list of dictionaries which will map *only active* qubits to states.

issue_title: Reading a XML BIF with isolated nodes
issue_body:
The XMLBIFReader class doesn't allow to return the model from a BIF XML file where there are isolated nodes. 

I can create the XMLBIFReader:
`bif = pgmpy.readwrite.XMLBIF.XMLBIFReader('diabetes.xml')`

but when trying to return the model:
`bif_model = bif.get_model()`

It returns an exception because is trying to load the CPD of the isolated node, but this node is not included in the BayesianModel:
 `ValueError: ('CPD defined on variable not in the model', <TabularCPD representing P(skin:1) at 0x7f669d905c10>)` arises.


Reviewing the  `XMLBIFReader.get_model()`, we can see that the Bayesian model is created from the edges: 

`model = BayesianModel(self.get_edges())`

As an isolated node has no edge by definition, I think that the BayesianModel can't know that an isolated node exists when adding the cpds to the BayesianModel.

I provide the following XML BIF as a MWE. It is a XML BIF learnt using WEKA BayesNet class.
[diabetes.zip](https://github.com/pgmpy/pgmpy/files/1449591/diabetes.zip)

I think that even when an isolated node is not the most useful thing to do, it is a nuisance that we can't load the rest of the network.

Execution environment.
pgmpy: 0.1.3
Python: 2.7
OS: Ubuntu 16.04

Some of you may have noticed that in the latest fontmake release [v1.2.3](https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/releases/tag/v1.2.3), I uploaded a stand-alone fontmake executable which bundles everything in a single file and does not even require Python to be installed.

I used the latest [pyinstaller](http://www.pyinstaller.org/) and Python 3.6.1 (official builds from Python.org) from my local machine, with OS X 10.10 and Windows 7 (64-bit).

The two zip files simply contain a `fontmake` (or `fontmake.exe` on Windows) plus the LICENSE file.
One simply can download it, unzip and run it in place, or move it anywhere in the shell's $PATH, like `/usr/local/bin`.

E.g., from the Mac bash one could do this:

```sh
curl -LO https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/releases/download/v1.2.3/fontmake-standalone-1.2.3-py36-macosx.zip
unzip fontmake-standalone-1.2.3-py36-macosx.zip
./fontmake --help
```

I thought that this could maybe lower the entry barrier and make fontmake more easily accessible for non Python developers, or those are not familiar with pip and virtual environments, etc.

If there's enough interest, I could set up Travis and Appveyor to automatically build the standalone executable upon tagged commits.

Note this is still experimental as I haven't tested it extensively, so I invite anyone who's interested in trying this out and report any issues.

Please let me know what you think. 
Thanks

issue_title: Description case is being changed in the meta-data generator
issue_body:
Somewhere in 'process_function_documentation' within 'documentation_support.py' in the internal metadata generators, we are changing case of function names. 

Examples of this include the changes shown in https://github.com/ni/nimi-python/pull/387/commits/6970ecb1a4a062449a37d6057c11e13c77d73c50

Additionally, all descriptions showing 'ViStatus' get changed to 'Vistatus'

In test_get_error_description_get_error_message
We set:

self.side_effects_helper['GetError']['return'] = -2

and this causes a warning to raise, but the test to pass.

We should add a new test to test this warning. The existing test should not have an error returned from get error.

issue_title: upgrading to autobahn v0.12.1 does not install pytrie
issue_body: I am using autobahn[asyncio] and when I either do a new install or pip install autobahn --upgrade, pytrie is not loaded. I have to manually load it.

* Configurable whether we output all (`--all`) three vectors or an average vector (`--average`).
* Able to output contextualized word vectors (input is a sentence) or uncontextualized word vectors (input is a word).
We expect PyTorch 0.3 to release on Monday 11/20.

issue_title: Install fails due to unavailable torch dependency
issue_body:
**Describe the bug**
`pip install allennlp` fails, beacuse it requires `torch==0.4.0`. Since July 26th PyTorch 0.4.1 is out, but PyTorch folks haven't published PyTorch 0.4.0 wheel yet (cf. pytorch/pytorch#9943) and also `pip install torch==0.4.0` fails. Hence you can't install allennlp from scratch now.

**To Reproduce**
`pip install allennlp` in an empty virtualenv

**Expected behavior**
AllenNLP installs.

**System (please complete the following information):**
 - OS: MacOS 10.13
 - Python version: Python 3.6 (also Python 3.7)
 - AllenNLP version: 0.5.1 (also master)
 - PyTorch version: 0.4.0

**Additional context**
Couldn't we make sure all tests pass under PyTorch 0.4.1 and update this requirement?

issue_title: Tests with exceptions marks as broken
issue_body:
[//]: # (
. Note: for support questions, please use Stackoverflow or Gitter**. 
. This repository's issues are reserved for feature requests and bug reports.
.
. In case of any problems with Allure Jenkins plugin** please use the following repository 
. to create an issue: https://github.com/jenkinsci/allure-plugin/issues
.
. Make sure you have a clear name for your issue. The name should start with a capital 
. letter and no dot is required in the end of the sentence. An example of good issue names:
.
. - The report is broken in IE11
. - Add an ability to disable default plugins
. - Support emoji in test descriptions
)

#### I'm submitting a ... 
  - [x] bug report
  - [ ] feature request
  - [ ] support request => Please do not submit support request here, see note at the top of this template.

#### What is the current behavior?
If test encountered any kind of exception, allure plugin marks him as broken.

#### If the current behavior is a bug, please provide the steps to reproduce and if possible a minimal demo of the problem
Test example:
```python
def test_stuff():
    raise Exception('whoa!')
```
JSON:
```json
{"name": "test_stuff", "status": "broken", "statusDetails": {"message": "Exception: whoa!", "trace": "test_4_stuff.py:14: in test_stuff\n    raise Exception('whoa!')\nE   Exception: whoa!"}}
```

#### What is the expected behavior?
If there is some exception in test, In `<...>-result.json` this test will have `"status": "failed"`.  
But if this exception was in, for example, fixture, there's definitely some weird things that needs to be marked as **broken**. 

#### What is the motivation / use case for changing the behavior?
I think having exceptions is _normal_ for Python. If in my test goes something wrong, it'll rather raise exception instead of returning None...

#### Please tell us about your environment:

- Test framework:        pytest@3.6.0
- Allure adaptor:        allure-pytest@2.3.3b1
- Python: 3.6.5

#### Other information 

[//]: # (
. e.g. detailed explanation, stacktraces, related issues, suggestions 
. how to fix, links for us to have more context, eg. Stackoverflow, Gitter etc
)
...but I'm not sure is it necessary to mark _all_ tests that have exceptions as _failed_.  
For example, if my test encountered some `MyApiException`, it's safe to assume that test is not broken, he's just failed. But if there is some serious stuff like `requests.exceptions.ConnectionError`, there's something wrong, because my test assumed that connection will be established and I could make request at least. 
IMHO, there could be some argument parameter like `--allure-exception=BaseException`, and if test was failed because of exception inherited of BaseException, test will be marked as failed. Otherwise test is **really** broken.

issue_title: Check package dependency by default and add a switch to bypass?
issue_body:
Currently we only check `R_libs` and `python_modules` dependencies when 1) a DSC is executed for the first time, and 2) new packages are added. But a corner case is when a user runs DSC, then remove the package required but do not touch the DSC file, then run DSC again -- DSC will not check for dependencies and will fail. 

I'm not sure if we should check dependencies by default and add an option to bypass the check if no new packages are added. This reminds me of `plink` 1.0.x always check for updates at the beginning which is so annoying that many people put `alias plink="plink --no-web"` in their bash profile ...

issue_title: Bunch of DeprecationWarnings in Python3 due to invalid escape sequences

Some scrapers still have potentially issue-prone regex patterns that could be an issue in Py3.7+. Guess I didn't catch these before.

Simple fix is to set these string literals to raw string literals.

issue_title: Out of disk space on machine running deis-builder
issue_body:
1 machine from the cluster (the one where `deis-builder` is running) runs out of space very quickly. 

We've figured out that it's `deis-builder-data` that dramatically eats space. And it grows fast with each deploy because `deis-builder` doesn't seem to fully clean the intermediate containers. 

The solution right now is to stop `deis-builder`, remove `deis-builder-data` container **with** its volume (`-v` option) and run `deis-builder` again:
```bash
# Log into the machine that is running deis-builder and:
deisctl stop builder
docker ps -a | grep deis-builder-data | awk '{print $1}' | xargs docker rm -v
deisctl start builder
```

issue_title: Add warning about encodings not supported by Python
issue_body:
We currently detected `EUC-TW` pretty well, but it's not actually supported by Python.  Most users would expect that

```python
result = chardet.detect(some_bytes)
try:
    some_bytes.decode(result['encoding'])
except UnicodeDecodeError:
    print('Oops. chardet detected the wrong encoding')
```

would always work, but the decode line can actually fail with a `LookupError` too because of encodings that aren't supported by Python.
