issue_title: Convert plugin API to be purely event based
issue_labels: [{'id': 815420353, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw4MTU0MjAzNTM=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/comp:%20trinity', 'name': 'comp: trinity', 'color': 'bfd4f2', 'default': False}, {'id': 983629919, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2Mjk5MTk=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/effort:%20E2%20days', 'name': 'effort: E2 days', 'color': 'bfe5bf', 'default': False}, {'id': 983616278, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2MTYyNzg=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/type:%20RFC%20/%20discussion%20/%20question', 'name': 'type: RFC / discussion / question', 'color': 'eeeeee', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### What things currently look like.

The current plugin API looks like this.

- `configure_parser`: Hook that can be implemented by plugin to modify CLI parser configuration
- `should_start`: Called by `PluginManager` to check whether a plugin should be started based on an *event*
- `start`: Called by the `PluginManager` if `should_start` returns `True` and the plugin has not been started yet.
- `stop`: Called by the `PluginManager` to stop plugins during shutdown.

The *Event* API works through the `PluginManager` and all events are fired by the `PluginManager`.  The following events are currenty available.

- `TrinityStartupEvent` - broadcast when the `NodeClass` is launched/started
- `PluginStartedEvent` - broadcast when a specific plugin is started
- `ResourceAvaiableEvent` - broadcast when a resource becomes available

This API has been a good start, but it has been built in direct response to the needs presented by the transaction pool and the other initial plugin experiments.  Now that we are starting to see what a plugin might look like, I propose the following changes to this API.

### What I think things should look like

First, I want to better define the initialization process and plugin lifecycle.

- `Plugin.bootstrap(arg_parser, sub_parser)`:
  - The very thing that is called on a plugin.  Lets experiment with making this a `classmethod` since this will occur early enough in the process that I don't think we can realistically initialize plugins at this stage.
  - This is where plugins get the chance to modify the CLI
- `Plugin.__init__(args, token)`:
  - Plugin classes get initialized.  
  - `args` is the CLI `Argparser` result.
  - `token` is a `CancelToken` that the plugin should use to gracefully handle shutdowns (not fully fleshed out).
- `Plugin.ready(chain_config, broadcast_queue, subscription_queue)`
  - Concept lifted from [django AppConfig.ready](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/ref/applications/#django.apps.AppConfig.ready)
  - Name subject to change
  - Concept is that this is the last point prior to things *starting*
  - `broadcast_queue` is an `asyncio.Queue` that the plugin can use to broadcast an event (more below)
  - `subscription_queue` is an `asyncio.Queue` that the plugin can consume events that have been broadcast by other plugins. (more below)
- `Plugin.run()`
  - Starts the plugin
- `Plugin.cancel()`
  - Called when the app is exiting.  Last chance for a plugin to gracefully shutdown.

After `ready` and before `shutdown` is when the app is running.  Within these boundaries, the Trinity application is running and plugins are *active*.  During this period, plugins interact via *events*.

Here is the best description of how I think events should work.

1. Events should probably be registered sometime during initialization.  This could happen automatically by the `PluginManager`.
  - `Plugin.get_broadcast_events()`: Returns all of the events that this plugin can broadcast.
  - `Plugin.get_subscription_events(registry):` Returns all of the events that this plugin wants to subscribe to.  The `registry` would be a data structure containing all of the registered events (and probably the plugin which registered them).
2. Plugins may broadcast any event that it registered from `get_broadcast_events` using the `broadcast_queue`
3. Plugins read events from the `subscription_queue` and respond.

We probably have a few *core* events that are provided by the plugin manager or some other built-in plugin.

- `DiscoveryStarted` to give plugins access to the `DiscoveryService`
- `PeerPoolStarted` to give plugins access to the `PeerPool`
- `PeerConnected` to give plugins info on connected peers.
- `PeerDisconnected` ... you get the point...

Currently we use this pattern to denote that `MyThing` will have a `prop_a` property.

```python
class MyThing:
    prop_a = None
```

It looks like if we instead use `__slots__` we could see 40-50% reduction in memory footprint.

```python
class MyThing:
    __slots__ = ["prop_a"]
```

source: 

- https://docs.python.org/3/reference/datamodel.html#slots
- http://book.pythontips.com/en/latest/__slots__magic.html

issue_labels: [{'id': 631592722, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw2MzE1OTI3MjI=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/Good%20First%20Issue', 'name': 'Good First Issue', 'color': '0e8a16', 'default': False}, {'id': 983629570, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2Mjk1NzA=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/effort:%20E1%20hours', 'name': 'effort: E1 hours', 'color': 'bfe5bf', 'default': False}, {'id': 983628532, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2Mjg1MzI=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/prio:%20P4%20nice%20to%20have', 'name': 'prio: P4 nice to have', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 983615867, 'node_id': 'MDU6TGFiZWw5ODM2MTU4Njc=', 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/ethereum/py-evm/labels/type:%20refactor', 'name': 'type: refactor', 'color': 'eeeeee', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
### What is wrong?

I noticed that our gas constants are a bit messy and should be reorganized.

Let's take `GAS_BALANCE` as an example which is defined as such:

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/bf1d3c2ad653b4ed6ab9ffb836735863bb5d8148/eth/constants.py#L43

For someone new to the code base this may seem as if retrieving the balance from some account has a fixed gas cost of `20` when in reality it is `400` as specified with the tangerine whistle fork.

https://github.com/ethereum/py-evm/blob/bf1d3c2ad653b4ed6ab9ffb836735863bb5d8148/eth/vm/forks/tangerine_whistle/constants.py#L13

### How can it be fixed

My gut feeling says that only *true constants*™ belong into `eth.constants` and that all *fork specific* constants should live in `evm.vm.forks.<fork_name>.constants`.

issue_title: Easier way to replace pending transactions
issue_body:
* Version: 4

### What was wrong?

Replacing a pending transaction takes too much boilerplate code. Much more convenient APIs are possible for common use cases like:

- bump the gas price of a pending transaction to new gas price P Gwei
- replace pending transaction A with pending transaction B at the minimum gas price increase
- replace pending transaction A with new transaction C

### How can it be fixed?

A new method like: `w3.eth.replaceTransaction`

```
var event = myContract.LogJob({}, {fromBlock:0, toBlock:'latest'});
```

issue_title: Upgrade to or add support for websockets v5
issue_body:
### What was wrong?

We are currently using the `websockets` library's v4 line.  The v5 line is out.

### How can it be fixed?

Look into adding support for both v4 and v5.

If this is too cumbersome, we can simply upgrade to requiring `>=v5`

Comments in #48 suggest that pyDelphin should have the capability to outright reject malformed *MRSs during deserialization. This should be accessible via a keyword argument to the load(s)(_one) methods, e.g.:

```python
simplemrs.loads(s, errors='strict')
```

Values of the `errors` parameter could be:
* `ignore` - try to load anyway
* `warn` - try to load, but warn the user
* `strict` - raise an exception on errors

If some argument already exists for similar behavior (e.g., `strict=True` for simplemrs), it should be deprecated.

This should be available for all deserializers. The `is_well_formed()` method of Xmrs should handle programatically constructed *MRSs.

issue_title: conda envs directories and package cache reversed from previous version causing errors
issue_body:
Hello!  Apologies in advance for the lengthy issue.  I am doing some regression testing of conda installs and noticed issues as I installed a test env using conda 4.3.11.  All my work with 4.3.9 has worked until this point; and I was hoping to keep on top of a regular conda refresh on the many machines I work on.

The install (some info omitted):
```
Current conda install:

               platform : linux-64
          conda version : 4.3.11
       conda is private : False
      conda-env version : 4.3.11
    conda-build version : not installed
         python version : 2.7.13.final.0
       requests version : 2.12.4
       root environment : /usr/platform/miniconda2  (read only)
    default environment : /usr/platform/miniconda2
       envs directories : /usr/platform/miniconda2/envs
                          /home/userid/.conda/envs
          package cache : /usr/platform/miniconda2/pkgs
                          /home/userid/.conda/pkgs
           channel URLs : <private>
            config file : /usr/platform/miniconda2/.condarc
           offline mode : False
```

I was attempting `/usr/platform/miniconda2/bin/conda create -n foobah python=3`, but was greeted with the error:
```
Traceback (most recent call last):
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/exceptions.py", line 616, in conda_exception_handler
        return_value = func(*args, **kwargs)
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 137, in _main
        exit_code = args.func(args, p)
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/cli/main_create.py", line 68, in execute
        install(args, parser, 'create')
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/cli/install.py", line 222, in install
        unknown=index_args['unknown'], prefix=prefix)
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/core/index.py", line 125, in get_index
        index = fetch_index(channel_priority_map, use_cache=use_cache)
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/core/index.py", line 552, in fetch_index
        repodatas = _collect_repodatas(use_cache, tasks)
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/core/index.py", line 541, in _collect_repodatas
        repodatas = _collect_repodatas_serial(use_cache, tasks)
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/core/index.py", line 514, in _collect_repodatas_serial
        for url, schan, pri in tasks]
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/core/index.py", line 144, in func
        res = f(*args, **kwargs)
      File "/usr/platform/miniconda2/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/core/index.py", line 500, in fetch_repodata
        with open(cache_path, 'w') as fo:
    IOError: [Errno 13] Permission denied: u'/usr/platform/miniconda2/pkgs/cache/82c9030b.json'
```

Upon some investigation of 4.3.9 vs 4.3.11 I noticed the resolution of package caches and envs dirs might be reversed from their defaults in 4.3.9 which would appear to have resulted in the above error.  Is this expected?  Were these reversed by-design in an intermediate release?

4.3.9 conda info snippet: 
```
envs directories : /home/userid/.conda/envs
                            /usr/platform/miniconda2/envs
package cache : /home/userid/.conda/envs/.pkgs
                          /usr/platform/miniconda2/pkgs
```
4.3.11 conda info snippet:
```
envs directories : /usr/platform/miniconda2/envs
                            /home/userid/.conda/envs
package cache : /usr/platform/miniconda2/pkgs
                          /home/userid/.conda/pkgs
```

For additional context: /usr/platform/miniconda2/envs is root-writable only, and we would like to keep it that way for many reasons.

Adding to that, I had a thought: the shared cache - while very helpful at dedup-ing packages on a single machine - is making it very difficult to create reproducible environments at scale due to some of the (implied?) strictness of the way caches and envs are resolved.  Would it be possible to do any or all of the following:
- introduce a new configuration in condarc that explicitly disables usage of the shared cache for users?
- break out the caches from the envs dirs in condarc?
- make conda look more closely at package caches and envs dirs - checking them for writable behavior, e.g. - before assuming they are OK to use?  OR simply make conda more passive - if one cache fails, silently ignore and attempt the next until all are exhausted?

I suggest the last of those because it appears a writability check could have salvaged the install by skipping to my home dir cache and would make the cache and envs dirs resolution order less relevant, allowing for an accepted "default" to be adhered to while allowing for users to proceed.

I'd love to hear suggestions on how to handle this or if any of the above are possible, as any or all of these would help greatly with platform management and might help prevent me from pulling the python install out from under the users' environments when we roll upgrades.

When adding other channels to my `.condarc`, I'm find that `conda` is adding `file://` channels for `conda-build` to the `.condarc`. This is problematic as these directories don't exist yet. As a result, `conda` is unable to work afterwards. Here's an [example log]( https://circleci.com/gh/nanshe-org/docker_nanshe/6?utm_campaign=vcs-integration-link&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github-build-link ) showing this failure.

issue_title: Argument `--copy` seems to be ignoring when create environment
issue_body:
Hello!

For several month ago when I created new environment, for example
```
conda create -p E:\JN\dist\pyenv36-win64 --copy python=3
```
I got a list of packages to be installed and see `(copy)` and the end of each line:

```
The following NEW packages will be INSTALLED:

    pip:            9.0.1-py36_1   (copy)
    python:         3.6.1-0        (copy)
    setuptools:     27.2.0-py36_1  (copy)
    vs2015_runtime: 14.0.25123-0   (copy)
    wheel:          0.29.0-py36_0  (copy)

Proceed ([y]/n)? y
```

Now I don't see `(copy)` and it seems for me that `--copy` argument is ignoring by conda now.

issue_title: Installing another user's shared environment on Wakari fails
issue_body:
My attempt to install a Wakari environment shared by another user failed with the following arcane messages in the shell terminal window:

[~]$
[~]$ conda clone -n wms_sample wms_sample/metadata/share-*.tar.bz2
An unexpected exceptional error has occurred, please consider sending the following traceback to the conda GitHub issue tracker at https://github.com/ContinuumIO/conda/issues

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/opt/anaconda/bin/conda", line 5, in <module>
    sys.exit(main())
  File "/opt/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/cli/main.py", line 121, in main
    args.func(args, p)
  File "/opt/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/cli/main_clone.py", line 44, in execute
    for w in clone_bundle(path, prefix):
  File "/opt/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/conda/builder/share.py", line 78, in clone_bundle
    assert not isdir(prefix)
AssertionError
[~]$

It seems that copy of the environment did not get created properly.

I use conda to get all the dependencies needed to develop a package and have an environment equivalent to what `conda build` would do. There is a way to get all the runtime dependencies:
```
conda install <package> --only-deps
```

which previously could be done with
```
conda install <package>
conda remove <package> --force
```
but that does not install the build dependencies. They are also cumbersome to find, because as far as I can see one has to look at the conda recipe.  Alternatively, failing to set them up correctly can lead to hard to debug compiler or linker errors (like the ones caused by the system `pkg-config` finding a slightly different library).

## Expected Behavior
<!-- What do you think should happen? -->

```
conda install <package> --only-build-deps
```

should install the build dependencies. Alternatively `--only-deps` should install both runtime and build time dependencies, since that's the most common use case I can think of.

I am using [powerline](https://github.com/powerline/powerline) to display the current anaconda environment in my shell. issue_title: Support use case when test cases are not located in test bench top level.
issue_body:
In VUnit it is preferred to always let the test bench contain the test cases on the top level and have it instantiate any fixtures rather than the other way around. We believe all test bench / test fixture pairs can be structured in this way without any negative effects.

However there might be many legacy test benches / fixture pairs structured in the opposite way.
To accommodate these legacy structures we will add a `test_bench.scan_tests_from(file_name)` method to the Python-API.

issue_title: [Discussion] Could the help text for the VNet create command be a little clearer?
issue_body:
Could the behavior of the `--subnet-prefix` parameter be clarified any further? It states that it will reserve a portion of the vnet's address space, but in my experience, it seems to use the following heuristic: `<vnet-prefix>/max(24, <vnet-cidr-block>)`.

Maybe the behavior isn't this simple, but if it is, it would be cool if the help text stated this, that way a user would know exactly when they do or don't need to explicitly set it (e.g. `If omitted, automatically reserves a `/24` (or as large as allowed) portion of the VNet address space, .`).

This isn't that high-pri, and maybe I'm just being nit-picky, but I had to try the `az network vnet create` command with and without the `--subnet-prefix` parameter set in order to see how it worked, which seems non-ideal.

Likely `"$vms_ids"` or `"${vm_ids}"` was intended. Variables with spaces in them should be quoted an a `$` is required for variable subsitution in Bash.

issue_title: [Storage] It should be possible to configure storage in the local storage emulator
issue_body:
### Description
**Overview**

I have some configuration scripts to configure my storage accounts, and upload certain data files to set storage containers. I also use the Azure Storage Emulator, and would like to be able to use the same scripts to configure both in an equivalent way.

This is possible in other azure tools, where you can use a connection string of "UseDevelopmentStorage=true", or by using the account name of "devstoreaccount1" and the key listed in [this documentation page](https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/storage/storage-configure-connection-string)

I would like to be able to do the same with the CLI tool in some way. I would be happy to use a flag of --local, or to have the connection-string parser understand "UseDevelopmentStorage=true", or even have the given account name and key combination be understood to mean to use the local storage emulation.

#### Expected Behaviour

I expected commands such as 
     ```az storage container list --account-name devstoreaccount1 --account-key Eby8vdM02xNOcqFlqUwJPLlmEtlCDXJ1OUzFT50uSRZ6IFsuFq2UVErCz4I6tq/K1SZFPTOtr/KBHBeksoGMGw==```

````ERROR:
issue_title: [Test] Errors thrown when "expect_failure=True" should not be displayed.
issue_body:
Tests that previously used code like `with self.assertRaises(...):` would suppress error messages on the principle that any red text displayed during tests should indicate a failure. 

These tests, with ScenarioTest, no longer work with this pattern and instead expect a kwarg `expect_failure=True`.  However, these commands do output the failures in red but the test passes.

`expect_failure=True` should also prevent these error messages from being displayed to adhere to the same principle.

During investigation of HTTP client exceptions on big installations, history can provide information about failure reason (incorrect redirect and etc).

## Expected behaviour

Accessible list `ClientResponseError().history`

HistoryRecord = namedtuple('HistoryRecord', ('url', 'code', 'reason', 'headers'))
ClientResponseError().history: typing.List[HistoryRecord]
```

So I’ve just got a bunch of warnings around make_handler being deprecated and I’ve completely failed at finding any information on it until I looked into GitHub and switched to the 3.2 branch or searched for Changelog in the docs.

Am I missing something or is there no canonical way to find it? I would suggest to put the link right next to `Current version is 3.2.1.` at the top, like I do with [my projects](http://www.attrs.org/en/stable/).

## Long story short

call set_compression() on a response object and return it to aiohttp server causes a RuntimeError ("Using chunked encoding is forbidden for HTTP/1.0")

## Expected behaviour

There should be at least two ways to use compression on HTTP/1.0:

1. Fully compress the content, and return the length of the compressed data in the Content-Length header;
2. Do not return Content-Length header, return the compressed stream and set Connection: close. Close the connection after transmit.

## Actual behaviour

An runtime error is raised, and the connection is closed immediately.

In fact, it seems that by default, even a HTTP/1.1 request is responded with a HTTP/1.0 response, so it always raises this exception.

## Steps to reproduce

Call enable_compression on any response object before returning.

## Comment

I think it is absolutely not correct to enable chunked encoding on compression. Content-Encoding is not a part of transfer, it is a part of the content. It should never affect the transfer encoding type.

    update    asyncpg     3      501      622           24%
    update    asyncpg     4      378      456           21%
    update    asyncpg     5      298      352           18%

 plaintext          -     1     7159     6592           -8%
 plaintext          -     2     6941     6408           -8%
 plaintext          -     3     6732     6080          -10%
 plaintext          -     4     4651     4655            0%
```

(All numbers are requests per second as given by their `results.json` output.  The different steps refer to number of queries executed for the DB tests and concurrency for the other tests. Apart from aiohttp, no other packages have changed.)

It seems that that is a general trend of ~10% performance regression however, the code using raw asyncpg queries are consistently much faster.

I know these tests are far from perfect but my questions are:
1. Would it be possible for aiohttp to have some internal performance benchmarks that are run regularly so it's easy to see the changes in performance of different versions? Thereby making it easier to maintain and improve performance as new features, checks & error handling are added.
2. What has caused the regression in performance with aiopg and with simple requests? Could it be reversed?

issue_title: Checkpoints.create_checkpoint() should have capability to work with Model also
issue_body:
Presently the Checkpionts class has `create_checkpoint(self, contents_mgr, path)` which works by reading the model from the given path.

As a result, code such as [this](https://github.com/ipython/ipython/blob/3.x/IPython/html/services/contents/filemanager.py#L387) has to issue another `get()` request to load the model, while it already has access to a model right there with it in main memory (Refer [this line](https://github.com/ipython/ipython/blob/3.x/IPython/html/services/contents/filemanager.py#L383)).

Checkpoints are mostly created when a notebook is already loaded, and in such cases the chances are we already have a `model` object available with us.

So it would be great if some mechanism such as `create_checkpoint_from_model()` is made available that uses any existing model data directly (without having to read from the path again). Perhaps an optional additional `model` argument to `create_checkpoint` should do fine.

issue_title: Current release version is not compatible with np.arctan2
issue_body:
Once I installed autograd from pip, and found that the simple code below gets a keyerror:

```
from autograd import grad
import autograd.numpy as np

def fffff(x):
    return np.arctan2(x[1],x[0])

grad(fffff)(np.array([1,1],dtype='float'))
```

But it seems that this has been fixed in the latest source code. So please consider to update the release version.

I've  been running into divide by zero issues when using `anp.power` with inputs equaling zero. This issue seems similar to #93. The [line](https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/master/autograd/numpy/numpy_grads.py#L77) in question seems to be:
```
anp.power.defgrad(lambda ans, x, y : unbroadcast(ans, x, lambda g :\
    g * y * x ** (anp.where(y, y - 1, 1.))))
```

If this is a bug, would the following modification suffice?
```
anp.power.defgrad(lambda ans, x, y : unbroadcast(ans, x, lambda g :\
    g * y * x ** (anp.where(anp.logical_and(y, x),  y - 1, 1.))))
```
If additional numerical stability is needed, `anp.logical_and(y, x)` could be swapped out for `anp.logical_and(y, anp.greater(anp.abs(x), const)` where `const` is some small positive constant, e.g., `10.0*anp.finfo(x.dtype).eps`.
````

Hi! At the moment twisted greenlet is being started in the plugin in autoused session-scoped fixture, which means **after** test generation. This doesn't allow us to await deffereds on earlier phases like `pytest_generate_tests` or `pytest_configure`. I wonder if you mind moving greenlet initialization a bit earlier in plugin registration process, e.g. to `pytest_configure` hook or which one is more appropriate (`pytest_addhooks`?), so we wouldn't need to do it manually by direct imports from the plugin. Thank you!

One feature that I often find myself wishing for is a command line switch to disable reruns entirely, even for tests marked with `@pytest.flaky(reruns=N)`. This would be useful when rewriting/updating tests without commenting/uncommenting the `@pytest.flaky` decorator.

issue_title: Concurrent checkouts lead to incorrect 'num_allocated' stocks
issue_body:
When two customers check out concurrently for the same product, only one of the checkouts is reflected in the num_allocated field.

Steps to reproduce:
1.  Start sandbox, create two customers, create/pick test product.
2.  Both customers add the product to their baskets and perform a checkout concurrently, making sure both customers finish checkout (click the Place Order button) as close to the same time as possible.

We have reproduced this behavior on a single machine, by performing the above steps with one customer in one browser window and another customer in a second browser window.

Expected Result:
The num_allocated for the product is 2.

Actual Result:
The num_allocated for the product is 1.

Due to the change in ad1094c1e8, if a line's tax is set to `Decimal('0.00')`, `Line.is_tax_known` will return `True`, but `Line.line_tax` will return `None`. Formerly in this case, `Line.line_tax` returned `Decimal('0.00')` instead of `None`. I'd suggest making `line_tax` check `is_tax_known` instead of doing it's own check.

```
 @property
     def line_tax(self):
-         if self.unit_tax:
+         if self.is_tax_known:
             return self.quantity * self.unit_tax
``` 

See [ad1094c1e8:src/oscar/apps/basket/abstract_models.py:828](https://github.com/django-oscar/django-oscar/commit/ad1094c1e89c0314c0a883b0b0a4e618a443da22#diff-519bc8e8997795240c2a90d8d63b66baL828)

issue_title: Visual studio 2017 can not stop dotnet.exe
issue_body:
# Visual studio 2017 can not stop dotnet.exe

Visual studio is unable to stop dotnet.exe process. 

# General

After migration of 3 dotnet core projects from dotnet core 1 to 2.0, Visual Studio cannot stop dotnet.exe processes after stopping the debugging. The solution contains 3 dotnet core projects (Identity server, Api and Web application). 

Visual studio shows the popup window "**_Stop Debugging in Progress..._**" with the message: "**_Debugging is being stopped but is not yet complete. You can force debugging to stop immediately, but any processes being detached may be terminated instead. this window will automatically close when the debugging has completely stopped._**" but it can not stop the dotnet.exe process.

Even the "End Process" on the Processes-Tab can not terminate the dotnet.exe process, it gives an AccessDenied warning.

Even the command "taskkill /pid ProcessID /f" cannot terminate it. It returns "_**The process with PID ProcessID could not be terminated.**_" Error, with "_**This task is not running currently**_" as reason.

The only possible solution that I have at this moment is restarting my pc, which makes the development impossible.

Does anyone have any idea why this is happening?
Please report any issues you find with .NET Core 1.0.1 here, either responding to this issue or creating a new issue.

At this point, I am stumped as to where to even look for the bottlenecks between the OS's. I'm stumped as to how a box that has much stronger hardware, hooked up to the same network, can cause such a drastic performance drop. Unfortunately, I cannot post up the code to share. 

Is there any documentation I can reference for possible GC differences between OS's, or maybe variances is TPL/Threading that core may or may not support on certain OS's?

issue_title: test_leauto_upgrades should be fixed for amazonlinux
issue_body: Currently, it fails because `letsencrypt-auto` didn't work on that platform for the 0.1.0 release.  So we should begin with the first working version on the amazonlinux AMIs

I do not want any prompts.

```letsencrypt certonly --webroot -w /var/www/html -d example.com -m mail@example.com --agree-tos```

brings me pretty far but if I have certificates in place and want to add more domains:

```letsencrypt certonly --webroot -w /var/www/html -d example.com,app.example.com -m mail@example.com --agree-tos```

I get an annoying prompt. Is there something like a ```-y``` flag?

$ sudo bash
Password:
bash-4.3# letsencrypt -d blahblah.local auth
/etc/letsencrypt exists, this client can't access it
bash-4.3# 

Even though /etc/letsencrypt exists, and is chmod 777'd, and I'm logged in as root.

Hello,

It seems that letsencrypt-auto update himself to the latest release of source files.

Is there a way to use letsencrypt-auto without automatic update to the latest version? 

If not, it will be great to have an option like `--release` with values release name found on https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/releases and default `auto`.

Thanks,

Arnaud

Command input: ```./letsencrypt-auto --apache --server https://acme-v01.api.letsencrypt.org/directory --agree-dev-preview```

issue_title: Cannot request Certs if I've requested for that domain before (Apache - Error: should only be one vhost in vhost file)
issue_body:
Hi there

I'm toying a bit around with Let's Encrypt. Basically I wanted to write a script that can be used with my config panel. For this I have several domains which I test on.

Now I think I have the script ready and is working, so I wanted to reset all of LE. However, after deleting all of LE that I could find, I just get an error, that there should only be one vhost in the apache vhost file.

Below, you'll see that I first clear all of LE. Then I get current git. Then I try to get certs for the domain 'sjau.ch'. I've used that domain before and I get that error. I also cat the according vhost file and it contains two entries. One for ipv4 and one for ipv6.

However, when I run LE for the domain "ensemen.ch" - which I haven't used before -, then it works. Also this vhost file contains multiple vhost entries, namle ipv4/6, ports 80 and 443.

issue_title: Update fails silently if outbound connection is blocked
issue_body:
letsencrypt-auto silently fails to update (on first startup) if no outbound connection can be established (for example because an outbound firewall is blocking the requests).

The only thing to be seen is

`Updating letsencrypt and virtual environment dependencies...`

before dropping back to a shell. Please add appropriate error messages in case a connection cannot be established, otherwise troubleshooting may become very hard.

Is this on my side only?Did you get this issue?

It is not clear to me that there is, currently, any good way to detect a non-existent time using dateutil time zones. I think maybe the right thing to do is to convert all dateutil time zones to use a common base class (`_common._tzinfo`) or mixin that implements some function for detecting non-existent and/or ambiguous times.

This will make it much easier to deal with, for example, an `rrule` that uses a `dateutil` time zone but which might generate a non-existent time. With `pytz`, you can already do something like this:

``` python
from functools import partial
import pytz
from dateutil import rrule

RR = list(rrule.rrule(freq=rrule.DAILY, dtstart=datetime(2002, 4, 6, 8, 30), count=2, byhour=2))

NYCp = pytz.timezone('America/New_York')

def datetime_exists(x, tzi):
    try:
        tzi.localize(x, is_dst=None)
    except pytz.AmbiguousTimeError:
        pass
    except pytz.NonExistentTimeError:
        return False

    return True

RR1 = list(filter(partial(datetime_exists, tzi=NYCp), RR))
```

Which filters out the non-existent dates. We should probably have some `is_ambiguous()` and `exists()` functions for `dateutil` timezones.

issue_title: dateutil does not accept a dt argument of None
issue_body:
Per the Python Standard Library Documentation in regards to a `tzinfo` objects `tzname(...)`, `dst(...)`, and `utcoffset(...)` methods:

> These methods are called by a datetime or time object, in response to their methods of the same names.  A datetime object passes itself as the argument, and a time object passes None as the argument. A tzinfo subclass’s methods should therefore be prepared to accept a dt argument of None, or of class datetime.

issue_title: Switch license to Apache 2.0 for all new work, add AUTHORS and CONTRIBUTING files
issue_body:
I think that the Apache 2.0 license has some significant advantages over the BSD license, namely that patents are explicitly dealt with and clause 5 makes it explicit that contributions to the main repo will be licensed under Apache 2.0 *unless otherwise specified*. We still need to keep BSD license around for anyone who has not agreed to relicense, but since Apache and BSD are compatible, it's not a problem for all work going forward to be under the Apache 2.0 license.

As part of this, I think we should also add an `AUTHORS` file, so that the copyright notice is more accurate. I'll compile an `AUTHORS` file and it will be explicit who has their contributions licensed under the old BSD license and who has their contributions licensed under Apache 2.0, and any author on the list can make a PR to add themselves to the Apache 2.0 relicense file. If we get to 100%, we'll drop the old BSD file.

issue_title: Workaround for handling SIGINT on Windows
issue_body:
Currently on Windows, because of the bug in Python, SIGINT signals are not caught properly inside asyncio and thus the process can only be terminated with SIGKILL or other signals, which is resulting an ungraceful termination and we cannot do anything after the termination.

Steps to reproduce:

in test.py:
```python
from telethon import TelegramClient 
from telethon import events

  client = TelegramClient("test", some_api_id, some_api_hash).start()

   @client.on(events.NewMessage()) 
async def handler(event): 
      await event.reply("Hello!")   

client.run_until_disconnected() 
print("hi")  // this never gets called
```

Run `python test.py`, process cannot be terminated with "CTRL+C"(SIGINT)

Reference for bug
https://github.com/python/asyncio/issues/407

Version
OS: tried on both Windows 7 & 10 x64,
Python: tried on v3.6.6 and v3.7.0 x64
Telethon: latest version on PIP 1.1.1

How I can see my recent channel actions with Telethon?
Thanks

I think it's easier to explain this issue with some pictures:

![2016-05-05-171533_1919x63_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8199224/15047395/10d67b4a-12e5-11e6-9aad-a7a604e70de9.png)

This happens with all the separators:

![2016-05-05-171707_1919x65_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8199224/15047414/2142f846-12e5-11e6-896e-937c9b1463e1.png)

It also happens with a different theme:

![2016-05-05-171850_1919x79_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8199224/15047446/4d08a624-12e5-11e6-9a56-51189ee62718.png)

And with `emacs -Q` as well:

![2016-05-05-172008_1919x60_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/8199224/15047490/802f0480-12e5-11e6-8d73-7cb495778161.png)

I am using latest `powerline` from MELPA, on GNU Emacs 25.0.93.1 (x86_64-debian-linux-gnu, GTK+ Version 3.14.5) of 2016-05-05. 

All I do is switching between windows with ALT+TAB, and upon returning on the Emacs windows this is what I see.

I found that using direct X to render the fonts will produce a much better result, I posted the results over at airlines, but I'm gonna copy paste them here, mostly because it will help to generate more accurate powerline symbols.


(I didn't find any topics on these in the issues, so I made one)
We know that the status bar even after font patching on windows will still is not perfect like you see on MacVim, so did a little searching, and I found that vim has rendering support for Direct X which is much better than the current rendering using GDI, but your vim has to support it, after a few tests, I have the following results:

Without Direct X (Status Bar)
![Status Bar Old](http://puu.sh/n8Ac8/ea6b69485e.png)

With Direct X (Status Bar)
![Status Bar New] (http://puu.sh/n8Ahw/caf5ff0f9a.png)

Without Direct X (Unicode Symbols from the Help)
![Unicode Sym old](http://puu.sh/n8Ae7/5dae6e1298.png)

With Direct X (Unicode Symbols from help)
![Unicode Sym New](http://puu.sh/n8AjA/4f4cc9a9fa.png)

Without Direct X (Powerline Symbols)
![Powerline sym old](http://puu.sh/n8Af5/26832dd4b0.png) 

With Direct X (Powerline Symbols)
![PowerLIne symbols New](http://puu.sh/n8Ako/5257a276c4.png)

This is the Vim I used: [Vim x64 with Direct X](https://bintray.com/veegee/generic/vim_x64/7.4.1403/view)
Thanks to Veegee we have the latest vim with Direct X enabled.

Also once you have Direct X support, you have to turn it on like the following:

    set encoding=utf-8   
    set rop=type:directx,gamma:1.0,contrast:0.5,level:1,geom:1,renmode:4,taamode:1

all of those options are adjustable, after a few tests, those worked for me, you can change them for yourself, but the last 3 options (geom, renmode, taamode) those I got the best result using those values.

I think with a few modifications to the font patching, we could get a much better result.
One disadvantage I noticed is it slows down vim a bit, not a lot, but noticeable.

So tell me what you think.

issue_title: Feature Request: Ability to sync data between 2 b2 buckets
issue_body:
It may be desirable at some point to make a new b2 bucket as a b2 'backup' before some destructive change to a local source replicating to a destination b2 bucket

To make this 'backup' on the b2 server side, the sync command could be used to replicate the data before making any local changes which may get replicated in an undesirable fashion.

The issue currently however, is that the b2 sync command expects 1 of the 'source' or 'destination' folders to be a local folder:

```
-bash-4.1# b2 sync b2://564dcdc2-4a25-fc44-af2a-b369d22dfa7d/* b2://564df3d9-26c9-bc34-6e42-a042e0948d28/
Traceback (most recent call last):                                 
  File "/usr/bin/b2", line 9, in <module>
    load_entry_point('b2==0.5.7', 'console_scripts', 'b2')()
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/b2/console_tool.py", line 873, in main
    exit_status = ct.run_command(decoded_argv)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/b2/console_tool.py", line 801, in run_command
    return command.run(args)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/b2/console_tool.py", line 621, in run
    max_workers=max_workers
  File "/usr/lib/python3.5/site-packages/b2/sync.py", line 897, in sync_folders
    raise ValueError('neither folder is a local folder')
ValueError: neither folder is a local folder
```

Both s3cmd and gsutil are capable of syncing between buckets server side.

issue_title: support for bucket info and lifecycle rules
issue_body:
The B2 service now supports storing bucket info with a bucket, and will soon support setting lifecycle rules to automatically delete old versions of files.

Any thoughts on what the interface to the command-line tool should be?

The obvious thing to do is to add `--bucket-info` and `--lifecycle-rules` options to the `create_bucket` and `update_bucket` operations.  Each would take JSON as an argument, like this:

```
b2 update-bucket --bucket-info '{"color": "blue"}' my-bucket
```

That seems reasonable, but may be inconvenient for storing large bucket info or a long list of lifecycle rules.  We could support pulling from a file, perhaps by using the curl convention of "@" to indicate a file.  (That will be unambiguous because JSON can't start with "@".)

```
b2 update-bucket --lifecycle-rules @rules.json my-bucket
```

Does this sound reasonable?

This has been a known bug for a while; finally posting it here.

`Mock` objects from the `mock` package (or Py3's `unittest.mock`) are not serialized correctly.

```python
from unittest.mock import Mock
from marshmallow import Schema, fields, pprint

class UserSchema(Schema):
    name = fields.Str()
    email = fields.Email()
    
schema = UserSchema()
mock_user = Mock()
mock_user.email = 'hi guys'
pprint(schema.dump(mock_user).data)
# {"name": "<Mock name='mock.name' id='4379527880'>", "email": null}
```

This question was raised in PR #239 

Citing @sloria 
> All "simple" fields (fields.Str, fields.Int, etc.) serialize None to None if allow_none=True. I'm not sure that fields.Boolean should be an exception...

Ideally we should get more feedback before removing this bug/feature from the field.

In class TodoSchema(Schema) function: [make_object](https://github.com/marshmallow-code/marshmallow/blob/12044877d8af3d8f07c39bf0c5df78d3d7a22df1/examples/peewee_example.py#L79), need to add @pre_load, otherwise, it won't return the object, instead, return the original JSON object.

# After

issue_title: TypeError: an integer is required during read operation
issue_body:
Hello,

I am writing a python driver for a USB serial device.

I'm using pyserial latest version on python 2.7.

I open the port like this:

```
self.serport = serial.Serial()
self.serport.port = self.port
self.serport.baudrate = self.baudrate
self.serport.timeout = self.timeout
self.serport.open()
```

And perform a series of read operations like this:

`data = self.serport.read(int(1)).decode()`

Some times, during normal operation, I get the following exception

```
Exception in thread Thread-7:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/ssd/buildroot/build_clb0005_po_nobk/target/usr/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 801, in __bootstrap_inner
  File "/home/ssd/buildroot/build_clb0005_po_nobk/target/usr/lib/python2.7/threading.py", line 754, in run
  File "/root/python/app/my_driver.py", line 101, in _read
    data = self.serport.read(int(1)).decode()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/serial/serialposix.py", line 457, in read
    buf = os.read(self.fd, size - len(read))
TypeError: an integer is required
```

Further investigation reveals the culprit to be the `self.fd` value being None, as evidenced by the following print statement:

```
type fd: <type 'NoneType'>
type size: <type 'int'>
type len read: <type 'int'>
```

Adding an exception handler with retry is apparently enough to fix this issue.

Any patch available for the serial library?

Thank you

issue_title: warning: field 'm_posix_timestamp_high' will be initialized after field 'm_posix_timestamp_low' [-Wreorder]
issue_body:
We cannot check for warnings in our header files and enable -Werror in part because some header files define constructors that attempt to initialize data members out of order:
```
/Users/jong/Source/turicreate/src/flexible_type/flexible_type_base_types.hpp:99:27: warning: 
      field 'm_posix_timestamp_high' will be initialized after field
      'm_posix_timestamp_low' [-Wreorder]
  inline flex_date_time():m_posix_timestamp_high(0), m_posix_timestamp_low(0),
                          ^
```
We should always write member initializer lists in the actual order of initialization, then enable -Wreorder.

issue_title: ModelChain+SingleAxisTracker aoi calculations are wrong, too easy to miscalculate AOI using SingleAxisTracker
issue_body:
tl;dr
----
A combination of bad API choices led to an AOI calculation problem when using ``SingleAxisTracker`` objects with ``ModelChain``. One possible solution requires minor changes to the ``SingleAxisTracker`` API and moving a few lines of code in ``ModelChain``.
 
Background
------------
The ``PVSystem`` initialization parameters ``surface_tilt`` and ``surface_azimuth`` are optional with default values of 0. Arguably, these should be required parameters for a ``PVSystem`` object, but that's not such a big deal. The problem comes with the ``SingleAxisTracker`` class. Because ``SingleAxisTracker`` inherits from ``PVSystem``, and because we don't do anything special to prevent this, ``SingleAxisTracker`` objects are also initialized with ``self.surface_tilt = 0`` and ``self.surface_azimuth = 0``. These ``SingleAxisTracker`` attributes should not exist or they should be ``None`` or ``nan``. 

The next problem occurs in the ``SingleAxisTracker.get_aoi`` method, which is simply inherited from ``PVSystem.get_aoi``. This ``get_aoi`` method relies on the ``self.surface_azimuth`` and ``self.surface_tilt`` parameters. That's a problem for ``SingleAxisTracker`` since we generally don't know these parameters at system construction time. A user could get the right answer by calculating the system's tracking data and then setting the angle attributes to the tilt and azimuth results, but that seems like a bad design and out of character with the rest of the library. It's especially problematic for ``SingleAxisTracker`` because its objects are initialized with tilt and azimuth values (0) that yield AOI numbers that might look reasonable to some at first glance. A better initialization method would have ensured that the ``SingleAxisTracker.get_aoi`` method failed or returned nans. 

The ``ModelChain.prepare_inputs`` method calculates AOI to support the AOI losses model (which has its own issues, unfortunately: #339). These [lines](https://github.com/pvlib/pvlib-python/blob/master/pvlib/modelchain.py#L760-L761) are wrong for ``SingleAxisTracker`` system objects. 

Solution
--------
I think the solution is to:

1. Implement a ``SingleAxisTracker.get_aoi`` method that requires ``surface_tilt`` and ``surface_azimuth`` parameters.
2. Move the ``ModelChain`` AOI calculation inside the ``if isinstance(self.system, SingleAxisTracker)`` [block](https://github.com/pvlib/pvlib-python/blob/master/pvlib/modelchain.py#L778).

This should be resolved in conjunction with #337.

issue_title: Breaks if called concurrently more than twice
issue_body:
Calling the script more than twice concurrently breaks the credential caching behaviour.

To reproduce:
```
ztoken & ; ztoken & ; ztoken &
```

I've come across this by trying to call `ztoken` from my concurrent service locally. I'm thinking this could be fixed by a having the script use a lock file to prevent itself from running more than once concurrently.

issue_title: Enable registration of OAuth2 Clients in compliance to RFC7591
issue_body:
Problem Description:
----
Using the implicit flow is nice but that does not give you a refresh token by default. The problem to be solve is that for tool like zign the client use the secure `authorization_code` [RFC-6749 Section 4.1](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-4.1) must be provisioned to each user. In doing so you are exposing the secret of the client rending the authentication towards the OAuth2.0 provider kind of useless. You can however use an access token of a user to create new client which is then bound to the employee, increasing not only the security of the refresh token out in the wild,
> Note: refresh tokens are bound to a client and can only be refreshed with the proper client

but that also ties the refresh token much closer to the individual user effectively personalizing a client just for that particular user.

Process Idea
----
1. Install zign
2. configure zign (endpoints)
3. Request access token via client using implict flow (returns access_token)
4. Use access_token to create client supporting auth_code (returns client)
5. Zign uses config to place received client in config dir
6. Zign initiates auth_code flow by opening the browser and requesting a code
7. The returned code is used to retrieve access_token and refresh_token

issue_title: Issues with quantification over several variables
issue_body:
I'm not sure that the problems I encounter are problems in Z3 per se, but I assume or at least hope that there are ways to avoid them. I'm looking for help in doing that.

My goal is to model the occurrence of an event over a period of time in order to be able to determine if it happens at a certain point that I'm asked about. For example, assuming that it is given that the event:
starts at  2000-05-12, 10:45:12, and
ends at:  2010-08-12, 14:53:52
If I'm asked whether it takes place at 2003-1-12 10:46:22, I should return 'yes'. 
If I'm asked whether it takes place at 2020-8-12 10:46:22, I should return 'no'. 

I define a predicate - f - of 6 variables - year, month, day, hour, minute and second - and asserts that it returns 'true' in the range of times when the event takes place, and 'false' outside of that range. 

(declare-fun f (Int Int Int Int Int Int) Bool)

(assert (forall ((y Int) (m Int) (d Int) (h Int) (mi Int) (s Int)) 
(=> (and 

(or 
(> y 2000) 
(and (= y 2000) (> m 5)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (> d 12)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (> h 10)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (= h 10) (> mi 45)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (= h 10) (= mi 45) (>= s 12)))

(or 
(< y 2010) 
(and (= y 2010) (< m 8)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (< d 22)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (< h 14)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (= h 14) (< mi 53)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (= h 14) (= mi 53) (<= s 52))) 

)

(= (f y m d h mi s) true))))

(assert (forall ((y Int) (m Int) (d Int) (h Int) (mi Int) (s Int)) 
(=> (or 
(< y 2000) 
(and (= y 2000) (< m 5)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (< d 12)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (< h 10)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (= h 10) (< mi 45)) 
(and (= y 2000) (= m 5) (= d 12) (= h 10) (= mi 45) (< s 12))) 
(= (f y m d h mi s) false))))

(assert (forall ((y Int) (m Int) (d Int) (h Int) (mi Int) (s Int)) 
(=> (or 
(> y 2010) 
(and (= y 2010) (> m 8)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (> d 22)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (> h 14)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (= h 14) (> mi 53)) 
(and (= y 2010) (= m 8) (= d 22) (= h 14) (= mi 53) (> s 52))) 
(= (f y m d h mi s) false))))

Then I can check whether the event takes place at a particular point that I'm asked about, for example, for 2003-1-12 10:46:22 I'm asserting:
(assert (f 2003 1 12 10 46 22))
This should result in SAT, which indicates that the event takes place at that point.

But for  2020-8-12 10:46:22:
(assert (f 2020 8 12 10 46 22))
I should get UNSAT, which indicates that the event doesn't take place at that point.

My problem is that even without the assertion about a specific point in time, it takes Z3 about 15 **minutes** to give a SAT answer. I don't know if this is a bug in one of its heuristics or an unavoidable limitation of being in an undecidable logic, but I noticed that if I reduce the modeling to 5 variables (i.e. avoiding seconds), it takes about 15 **seconds** to give a SAT answer, and for 4 variables (avoiding minutes and seconds) it's almost immediate. So the processing time is definitely related to the number of variables being quantified, but I have to model all 6 variables.

I'm trying to find ways to optimize my modeling or Z3 settings in order to get a quicker response. As for Z3 - I tried various settings such as:
(set-option :smt.mbqi true)
(set-option :model.compact true)
which didn't make a difference, and
(set-option :smt.mbqi false)
which causes Z3 to return 'unknown' immediately. 

I'd really appreciate any feedback and suggestions how to optimize this modeling or how to configure Z3 for obtaining a quicker response in this use-case.

Attached: a script with 5 variables that returns SAT in 15 seconds and a script with 6 variables that returns SAT in about 15 minutes.

[tests.zip](https://github.com/Z3Prover/z3/files/269106/tests.zip)

I am trying some examples in the QF_FP theory and encountered some strange behaviours when using NaN. (I have looked up the issues but could not find anything related to this problem, so I am posting this new issue).

I am running Z3 version 4.6.0 - 64 bit, MacOSX.

1. Converting a NaN with a signbit equals to 1 to a bv 
```
(set-option :produce-models true)
(set-logic QF_FPBV)
(set-info :source "Negative NaN")

```
produces
```
sat
((c1 #b0)
 (c2 #xff)
 (c3 #b00000000000000000000001))
```
Both signbit (c1) and significand (c3) have been "normalised" to an internal representation of NaN.

2. The following code
```
(assert
  (and
    (= nan1 (fp #b0 #b11111111 #b10000000000000000000001))
``` 

The IEEE standard defines NaNs as 's' '1......1' 'xxxxxxxxxxx' with one x not 0, which allows NaN with a signbit set to 1.

issue_title: -- Lasagne / Theano crash on new custom layer
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm experiencing a crash when using a simple lasagne custom layer as defined below.

**(1) my config:**
Windows 10
Python Anaconda 3.5
Theano 0.9.0beta1dev
Lasagne 0.2dev1

**(2) the custom layer code**
```python
class FoldpixelLayer_div2_pack(Layer):
    def __init__(self, incoming, W=init.Normal(0.25), **kwargs):
        super(FoldpixelLayer_div2_pack, self).__init__(incoming, **kwargs)
        self.num_inputs = self.input_shape[1]
        self.W = self.add_param(W, (self.num_inputs, 4), name='W')
    
    def get_output_shape_for(self, input_shape):
        kin = input_shape[1]        # nb input  features planes
        self.kout= kin              # nb output features planes
        return (input_shape[0], self.kout, int(input_shape[2]/2), int(input_shape[3]/2))

    def get_output_for(self, input, deterministic=False, **kwargs):
        out = T.zeros((input.shape[0], self.output_shape[1], self.output_shape[2], self.output_shape[3]), dtype=T.config.floatX)
        for quad in range (self.num_inputs):
            p1 = input[:,quad,0::2,0::2] * self.W[quad,0]
            p2 = input[:,quad,0::2,1::2] * self.W[quad,1]
            p3 = input[:,quad,1::2,0::2] * self.W[quad,2]
            p4 = input[:,quad,1::2,1::2] * self.W[quad,3]
            ps=  p1 + p2 + p3 + p4
            out = T.set_subtensor(out[:,quad,:,:], ps)
        return out      
 ```

**(3) symptoms:**
The code compiles ok both in cpu and/or gpu modes
Then it crashes python at execution with no further warnings... :(

I'm surely missing something!
Thanks for your help

```
class DropoutLayer(Layer):
         def get_output_for(self, input, deterministic=False, **kwargs):
               input_shape = self.input_shape
```
It's not safe to get input_shape from self.input_shape (input_shape = self.input_shape), when you define the input to propagate through the network instead of using the input variable associated with the network's input layer.

To illustrate my point, assuming I defined my DropoutLayer with code:
```
myDenselayer = DenseLayer(InputLayer((2,2)) # shape= 2 x 2
myDropoutlayer = DropoutLayer(myDenselayer)
```
And, I invoke above with code:
```
myInput = T.matrix() # shape= 3 x 3
helper.get_output(myDropoutlayer, myInput)
```
In above context, self.input_shape (2 x 2) have a  different value from myInput's shape (3 x 3).

Is it necessary to send `GET` request to S3 every time the file is uploaded? 

In methods `S3FS.setbinfile()` and `S3FS.setbytes()`, it sends two GET requests. First time, it checks if the the parent directory exists (calls `S3FS.isdir()`).  Second time, it checks if the URL is not a directory (calls `S3FS.getinfo()`).  

These operations are quite expensive. The effective upload speed increases at least x3 times when these methods are not called.

Here is the existing code:

    def setbinfile(self, path, file):
        _path = self.validatepath(path)
        _key = self._path_to_key(_path)

        if not self.isdir(dirname(path)):
            raise errors.ResourceNotFound(path)
        try:
            info = self.getinfo(path)
            if info.is_dir:
                raise errors.FileExpected(path)
        except errors.ResourceNotFound:
            pass

        with s3errors(path):
            self.client.upload_fileobj(file, self._bucket_name, _key)

Proposed solution:

    def setbinfile(self, path, file):
        _path = self.validatepath(path)
        _key = self._path_to_key(_path)

        with s3errors(path):
            self.client.upload_fileobj(file, self._bucket_name, _key)


And similar changes should be applied to `setbytes()` method.

issue_title: No Page models can be left without translation
issue_body:
In some projects not all models that extend `Page` should be translated.
In 0.6.0 all Page models that require translation had to be `register`ed in `translation.py`.
Since 0.8.0 if any model that extends `Page` has to be left without translation, there's an exception of the sort:
```
django.core.exceptions.FieldError: Unknown field(s) (seo_title_bg, search_description_en, seo_title_en, search_description_bg, slug_en, slug_bg) specified for ThePageModelThatShouldntBeTranslated
```
I suggest that only the models `register`ed in `translation.py` should be considered for translation.
The models that are not registered in `translation.py` can be filtered in this function.
https://github.com/infoportugal/wagtail-modeltranslation/blob/master/wagtail_modeltranslation/patch_wagtailadmin.py#L494

issue_title: Из документации не понял как пропускать некоторые части предложения при разборе
issue_body:
Например у меня есть:

"Привет! Вышлите плиз отчет по клиенту Трололо за июль 2017"

Структура у меня такая 
```
EntityFact = fact(
    'EntityFact',
    ['do', 'action', 'entity', 'name', 'date']
)
```
Т.е. 
do = вышлите
action = отчет
entity = по клиенту
name = Трололо
date = июль 2017

Часть кода, который разбирает это
```
class DoPipeline(MorphPipeline):
    grammemes = {'Do'}
    keys = [
        'скиньте',
        'вышлите',
        'дайте',
        'сделайте',
        'пришлите'
    ]


class ActionPipeline(MorphPipeline):
    grammemes = {'Action'}
    keys = [
        'статистика',
        'отчет',
        'закрывающие документы',
        'закрывашки',
        'акты'
    ]
```

Как мне сделать так, чтобы парсер не учитывал неизвестные ему части предложения между "do" и "action". 
Т.е. чтобы он фактически не воспринимал ничего лишнего между "Вышлите" и "отчет", сейчас всякие "плиз", "быстрее" и пр делают так, что парсер отказывается парсить.
 Тоже самое с датами - люди пишут "... Трололо за ДАТА" или просто "... Трололо ДАТА" при этом не хочется вести список разрешенных предлогов.
  
Есть варианты?

issue_title: No playback, mpsyt returns to the list of found records.
issue_body:
Mpsyt goes back to the list of found videos without playing anything.

## Issue
When I try to play any of the video/audio then mpsyt tries to get the file, but the moment it should run the player it goes back to the result list. I tried mpv/mplayer/vlc players and none of them works.

Please advise how to debug the issue.

## Your Environment
OS: Debian 8.10 Jessie
mpsyt: v0.2.8
mpv: 0.8.3
vlc: 2.2.8

When using DayNightCompositor and produce an image in a global EPSG:4326 grid there's a unmasked (black) area around the valida data area. If the sub-images used within the DayNightCompositor are loaded, resampled and saved, they have the areas masked correctly.

**To Reproduce**

areas.def:
```
REGION: EPSG4326 {
   NAME:    Global equal latitude/longitude grid for global sphere
   PCS_ID:  EPSG:4326
   PCS_DEF: init=EPSG:4326
   XSIZE: 8192
   YSIZE: 4096
   AREA_EXTENT:  (-180.0, -90.0, 180.0, 90.0)
};
```
$PPP_CONFIG_DIR/composites/seviri.yaml:
```yaml
  natural_with_night_fog:
    compositor: !!python/name:satpy.composites.DayNightCompositor
    standard_name: natural_with_night_fog
    prerequisites:
      - natural
      - night_fog
```

```python
import glob
from satpy import Scene

fnames = glob.glob('/data/*201805220700*')
glbl = Scene(reader='hrit_msg', filenames=fnames)
glbl.load(['natural_with_night_fog'])
lcl = glbl.resample('EPSG4326', resampler='nearest')
lcl.save_dataset('natural_with_night_fog', '/tmp/natural_with_night_fog.png')
```

**Expected behavior**
Image with properly masked out-of-area, like in the image below (composite from 0.6 channel, doesn't matter which one used).
![vis06](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3170788/43452932-55594616-94c1-11e8-812f-40005dcab2bd.png)

**Actual results**
Unmasked area around the valid data shouldn't be there.
![natural_with_night_fog_scaled](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/3170788/43453015-93a69aea-94c1-11e8-856f-39ed314ddad0.png)

**Environment Info:**
 - OS: Linux, RHEL6, Conda, Python 3.6
 - SatPy Version: 0.9.1a0.dev0
 - PyResample Version: 1.10.1

```python

debug_on()

comp_names = ['true_color', 'true_color_land']
scn.load(comp_names)

areaid = "mediterranean_east"
lcn = scn.resample(areaid, mask_area=False, radius_of_influence=5000)
lcn.show('true_color')

if TEST:
    x = lcn['true_color'].copy()
    composite_name = "true_color_land_and_sea"
    x.attrs['name'] = composite_name

    x1 = x.values
    land_rgb = lcn['true_color_land'].values
    mask = False
    x1[:, mask] = land_rgb[:, mask]

    lcn[composite_name] = xr.DataArray(x1, dims=lcn['true_color'].dims,
                                       coords=lcn['true_color'].coords,
```

issue_title: print resulting version string upon successfully running the write subcommand
issue_body:
A user will typically want to make sure the new version string is correct / looks good. So it is very likely that the user will run the `report` subcommand right after running the `write` subcommand like this:

![screenshot at 2017-11-20 22 08 37](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/213676/33047913-6187d0cc-ce3f-11e7-888b-07722744ccc9.png)

Because of that I'd suggest the success message for the `write` subcommand to print something like this instead:
    Version string successfully updated to: "Version 1.006; RELEASE"

So that there's no need to use `report` right after `write`.
How are we getting a certificate for the nightly tests? Credential problems seem to be the reason for their continued failure.

Hi all,
I tried to update to the newest ganga version on lxplus with the atlas setup on cvmfs but I got some problems with classes when using execfile.
What I do is setting up the ATLAS environment: 
`source ${ATLAS_LOCAL_ROOT_BASE}/user/atlasLocalSetup.sh`
then setting up the ganga verison (which is currently 6.2.2): 
`localSetupGanga`
setting up python over cvmfs (which is currently python 2.7.4-x86_64-slc6-gcc48) : 
localSetupPython
and then starting ganga.
I did a minimal setup where the problem can be seen. The files needed are saved here: 
https://cernbox.cern.ch/index.php/s/bCeCYcEmhR0XBSl
When doing
`execfile("userCode.py")`
The TestUtils.py are executed but when calling 
`methodCall()`
the class is not known. So it seems that the class definition is not propagated when using execfile.

Is it intended that this will not work in newer versions of ganga ? 

Cheers,

Draft for Q&A (will be copied to ipython-notebook)

#### pytraj developers don't recommnd to use traj.xyz  (as a numpy 3D array with shape=(n_frames, n_atoms, 3))?

Because `Trajectory` object in `pytraj` is a wrapper of C++ vector<Frame> in `cpptraj`. Everytime calling `traj.xyz` method, a copy of 3D array is made. User can not inplace update coords of each frame or specific atoms. Instead using traj[index] to do this. However, `traj.xyz` is still available to conviently access read-only data.

```python
traj['@CA'] = xyz # inplace update `xyz` coords of all CA atoms
```

So why not designing `xyz` as `mdtraj` with numpy? Because `cpptraj` already has more than few hundreds types of action/analysis combination (from things very simple like calculating distance, dihedral, ... to very smart 'autoimage' action). If we design like `mdtraj`, we need to copy data from `Frame` object (cpptraj) to numpy data. If we want to use `cpptraj`'s action/analysis, we again need to copy data from numpy back to `Frame` object.

We DO have numpy-backend for simple math for traj
```python

traj += traj2
traj -= traj2
traj /= traj2
traj *= traj2

traj.apply(lambda x : x + 100.) # `x` is `xyz` coords of each frame
```

issue_title: Padding is applied to all versions including manual ones
issue_body:
Apologies if this has been addressed elsewhere. I've started using Credstash, I am using text based versions that represent git tags plus CI job number, for example v1.0.0-1, which would be application version 1.0.0 build number 1.

I found straight away that even when using the -v flag when putting a secret into a fresh Dynamo table all my versions are left padded, so the example given above ends up as 00000000000v1.0.0-1. 

Digging through the docs and PRs related to the addition of padding I was under the impression that left padding is supposed to only apply to auto versioned variables, however digging into the code I can see that it is applied to all put operations - https://github.com/fugue/credstash/blob/master/credstash.py#L304

In fact it is applied twice to auto versioned variables as the version is padding is applied in the putSecretAction function just before the call to putSecret -
 https://github.com/fugue/credstash/blob/master/credstash.py#L380

The result of all this is that I am forced to left pad my versions when performing get operations, so if I want to look up version v1.0.0-1 I have to pass the left padded version (00000000000v1.0.0-1).

Is this behaviour intentional? I can see there is some documentation in the Readme but it seems to imply that left padding applies to auto versioning only.

issue_title: The clone/restoreState() actually includes the pseudorandomness of Atari.
issue_body:
The document said that clone/restoreState() **doesn't include** pseudorandomness. However, the fact is that clone/restoreState() **doesn't include** the _pseudorandomness of repeat_action_, but it **includes** the _pseudorandomness of Atari_.

The code to verify above fact is as below:
```
import numpy as np
import atari_py

test = 'loadROM'
# test = 'restoreState'

frame_skip = 4
bunch = 200
sequence = 500

def main():
    result = {
        'name':[],
        'grouped_num':[],
        'distribution':[],
    }

    # game_list = ['air_raid-n', 'alien', 'amidar', 'assault', 'asterix', 'asteroids', 'atlantis']
    # game_list = ['bank_heist', 'battle_zone', 'beam_rider', 'berzerk-n', 'bowling', 'boxing', 'breakout', 'carnival-n']
    # game_list = ['centipede', 'chopper_command', 'crazy_climber', 'demon_attack', 'double_dunk']
    # game_list = ['elevator_action-n', 'enduro', 'fishing_derby', 'freeway', 'frostbite', 'gopher', 'gravitar']
    # game_list = ['hero', 'ice_hockey', 'jamesbond', 'journey_escape-n', 'kangaroo', 'krull', 'kung_fu_master']
    # game_list = ['montezuma_revenge-n', 'ms_pacman', 'name_this_game', 'phoenix-n', 'pitfall-n', 'pong', 'pooyan-n']
    # game_list = ['private_eye', 'qbert', 'riverraid', 'road_runner', 'robotank', 'seaquest', 'skiing-n']
    # game_list = ['solaris-n', 'space_invaders', 'star_gunner', 'tennis', 'time_pilot', 'tutankham', 'up_n_down']
    # game_list = ['venture', 'video_pinball', 'wizard_of_wor', 'yars_revenge-n', 'zaxxon']

    game_list = ['pong', 'assault','ms_pacman']

    for game in game_list:

        if '-n' in game:
            '''games that are not in the nature DQN list'''
            continue

        game_path = atari_py.get_game_path(game)

        env_father = atari_py.ALEInterface()
        env_father.setFloat('repeat_action_probability'.encode('utf-8'), 0.0)
        env_father.setInt(b'random_seed', 3)
        env_father.loadROM(game_path)
        env_father.reset_game()

        if test in ['restoreState']:
            state_after_reset = env_father.cloneState()

        '''generate a sequence of actions'''
        action_sequence = np.random.randint(
            len(env_father.getMinimalActionSet()),
            size = sequence,
        )

        bunch_obs = []
        distribution = []
        samples = []
        for bunch_i in range(bunch):

            env_temp = atari_py.ALEInterface()
            env_temp.setFloat('repeat_action_probability'.encode('utf-8'), 0.0)
            env_temp.setInt(b'random_seed', bunch_i)
            if test in ['loadROM']:
                env_temp.loadROM(game_path)
                env_temp.reset_game()
            elif test in ['restoreState']:
                env_temp.loadROM(game_path) # restoreState without calling loadROM first will cause Segmentation fault (core dumped)
                env_temp.restoreState(state_after_reset)

            # just to make sure
            env_temp.setFloat('repeat_action_probability'.encode('utf-8'), 0.0)
            env_temp.setInt(b'random_seed', bunch_i)

            for sequence_i in range(sequence):
                for frame_skip_i in range(frame_skip):
                    env_temp.act(
                        env_father.getMinimalActionSet()[
                            action_sequence[sequence_i]
                        ]
                    )
                if env_temp.game_over():
                    env_temp.reset_game()

            obs = env_temp.getScreenRGB2()

            samples += [obs]
            found_at_bunch = -1
            if_has_identical_one = False
            max_value = 0
            for bunch_obs_i in range(len(bunch_obs)):
                obs_in_bunch = bunch_obs[bunch_obs_i]
                max_value = np.max(
                    np.abs(
                        obs-obs_in_bunch
                    )
                )
                if max_value < 1:
                    found_at_bunch = bunch_obs_i
                    if_has_identical_one = True
                    distribution[found_at_bunch] += 1
                    break

            if if_has_identical_one is False:
                bunch_obs += [obs]
                distribution += [1]

        grouped_num = len(bunch_obs)
        print('game:{} grouped_num:{} distribution:{}'.format(
            game,
            grouped_num,
            distribution,
        ))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
```

Specifically, when set ```test = 'loadROM'```, it tests the stochasticity produced by
```
setInt(b'random_seed')
loadROM()
```
The results are:
```
game:pong grouped_num:1 distribution:[200]
game:assault grouped_num:57 distribution:[4, 4, 6, 3, 8, 9, 2, 5, 3, 2, 5, 5, 5, 4, 5, 4, 2, 4, 6, 3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 5, 1, 2, 1, 3, 6, 3, 4, 2, 3, 5, 3, 4, 6, 3, 5, 4, 3, 2, 2, 3, 4, 2, 1, 3, 4, 3, 1, 2, 2, 5, 2, 1]
game:ms_pacman grouped_num:1 distribution:[200]
```

When set ```test = 'restoreState'```, it tests the stochasticity produced by ```clone/restoreState()```. The results are:
```
game:pong grouped_num:1 distribution:[200]
game:assault grouped_num:1 distribution:[200]
game:ms_pacman grouped_num:1 distribution:[200]
```

As you can see, we set repeat_action_probability to zero all the time since we do not care about this kind of stochasticity. The results shows that ```clone/restoreState()``` actually includes the pseudorandomness of Atari. Thus, we actually **do not** have a function that really exclude the pseudorandomness so that we can use it for planning.

Any ideas? Is there any way we can have a function that clone/restore state of Atari without pseudorandomness so that we can use it for planning?

issue_title: setup.py forces CPU version of tensorflow to be installed.
issue_body: Even if I already have tensorflow-gpu installed, running "pip install -e ." will install CPU version and override existing installation.

```
    surr1 = ratio * atarg # surrogate from conservative policy iteration
    surr2 = U.clip(ratio, 1.0 - clip_param, 1.0 + clip_param) * atarg #
    pol_surr = - U.mean(tf.minimum(surr1, surr2)) # PPO's pessimistic surrogate (L^CLIP)
```

`atarg` is just a `tf.placeholder`. So when the ratio term deviates from 1.0 by too much, isn't it causing the gradient to be zero? Because I think both `1.0 - clip_param` and `1.0 + clip_param` are not `tf.Variable`s.

issue_title: Django User model one-to-one relationship query error
class Input not being used on mutation with query variables

issue_body:
Hi there!

I was looking into implementing the functionality that [Warden](https://github.com/rmosolgo/graphql-ruby/blob/master/guides/schema/limiting_visibility.md) gives to graphql-ruby. Basically based on the context at execution time, I would like to hide certain schema elements from the user in queries, mutations, and introspection queries. 

What would be the suggested/best way to go about this? Should it be done in a custom Executor class?

Also, would a feature like this be on the roadmap for this framework?

Some additional discussion:

* https://github.com/rmosolgo/graphql-ruby/issues/269
* https://github.com/graphql/graphql-js/issues/113

Thanks!

`NonNull` and `List` are no longer listed in the latest version of the documentation.
http://docs.graphene-python.org/en/latest/types/scalars/

Also, an older documentation appears for 1 second and disappears:
http://graphene-python.org/docs/basic-types/

What is going on? Are `NonNull` and `List` being deprecated?

issue_title: Querying based on value of ReferenceField

issue_body:
https://github.com/graphql-python/graphql-core-next/blob/4d3b7824fc16554fea48f29b8a2a617e760056d6/graphql/subscription/map_async_iterator.py#L27-L41

This race condition becomes especially apparent when the subscription is being used in an "async-iterable" sort of mode:
```python
received = []
def _receive(subscription):
    async for result in subscription:
        received.append(result)
task = loop.create_task(_receive())

# ...
await subscription.aclose()

# e.g. ... await asyncio.sleep(.1)
assert not task.done()

# might I suggest using something like a SENTINEL which isn't passed through...
# here, I'm manually setting it, but you could set it, listen for it on EventEmitter or
# EventEmitterAsyncIterator
SENTINEL = object()
subscription.iterator.queue.put_nowait(SENTINEL)
await asyncio.sleep(.1)

assert task.done()
```

issue_title: Incorrectly parse versions of some apps
issue_body:
I saw that for `django-celery-beat` and `django-celery-results` it shows extra dots at the end of the version:
```
django_celery_beat	Django_Celery_Beat	1.0.1..
django_celery_results	Django_Celery_Results	1.0.1..
```

This is because both apps use custom `namedtuple`:
```
version_info_t = namedtuple('version_info_t', (
    'major', 'minor', 'micro', 'releaselevel', 'serial',
))
```

When `releaselevel` and `serial` are not set they are empty strings. This will lead to joining dots in the string representation.

I'm preparing a fix and will create a PR.
Since we no longer need access to type objects at `@param` decoration time, TypeSystem can wait to lazily import the types until they are first needed to improve start time.

issue_title: Ask conan.io for a InvalidConfigurationException (if it is useful)
issue_body:
Motivation.- Protobuf package has an issue with windows/shared configuration so [we have to disable it](https://github.com/bincrafters/conan-protobuf/blob/stable/3.5.1/conanfile.py#L35) and [prevent CI to run those jobs](https://github.com/bincrafters/conan-protobuf/blob/stable/3.5.1/build.py#L13) in order to get a green badge.

What to ask for.- I think that it would be useful to have a `conan.errors.InvalidConfigurationException` (better name?) to be raised from `configure()` method in recipes instead of the generic `ConanException` ([as it is suggested here](http://docs.conan.io/en/latest/uploading_packages/bintray/conan_center_guide.html#recipe-quality)). This way users will get the proper information when running conan, but projects like conan-package-tools can catch this specific exception, handle it and mark the job in CI as successful.

This way we don't need to tweak `build.py` files to avoid running certain configurations, so we move forwards to a even more standarized (unneeded) file.

I had some weird bug where this `concurrent.futures` library was shadowing the python3.5 built-in library which led me here. Although I can't repro my original problem anymore it seemed strange that this package depends on `futures`.

* safety version: master, 1.7.0  , 0.5.1
* Python version: any
* Operating System: any

### Description

Somewhat related to https://github.com/pyupio/safety/issues/90 , safety has a dependency on pip which is not declared in the requirements.

Instead `requirements_dev.txt` has `pip==9.0.1`

Currently the listed dependencies are
- Click>=6.0
- requests
- packaging
- dparse>=0.2.1

dparse `master` depends on pipenv, which depends on pip, but dparse latest release 0.2.1 does not include pip as a dependency, so on a brand new install of Python, running setup.py results in a missing dependency, unless `ensurepip` is used.  Not a very likely scenario, but still not desirable.

But more importantly, it is nice to declare dependencies in the package metadata.  Especially when that dependency has forever said that it isnt a library to be imported ;-)
