Support for this feature needs to be added to ncp_base and other associated source.

issue_title: DataRequest Poll Timer can become stuck
issue_body:
The logic in HandlePollTimer and SendMacDataRequest is such that it is possible for the mPollTimer to not be re-started when it should.

If SendMacDataRequest exits with error kThreadError_Already because a DataPoll is already in the SendQueue then the poll timer will not be re-started and the system will not continue to periodically poll.

The solution should likely have all the timer restart logic inside HandlePollTimer() rather than splitting it between that method and SendMacDataRequest.

issue_title: Implementar función para cargar un dataset del catálogo a un CKAN / Andino vía API
issue_body:
*Contexto*

La federación de datasets consiste en leer la metadata de un dataset de un nodo (que publica un data.json válido) y re-escribirla en otro nodo a través de la API (CKAN / Andino).

La administración ordenada de tareas de federación crónicas de muchos datasets y muchos nodos implica otras funcionalidades más complejas, pero que en última instancia requieren poder automatizar ese paso fundamental.

*Propuesta*

1. Implementar un nuevo módulo en pydatajson con las funcionalidades necesarias para escribir en la API de CKAN *todos los metadatos de un dataset* según el perfil de metadatos v1.1 de la guía (http://paquete-apertura-datos.readthedocs.io/es/stable/guia_metadatos.html#estandar-usado)

2. Integrar el uso de ese módulo en una función bindeada en el objeto `DataJson` del `core.py` de la librería que se llame `push_dataset_to_ckan(dataset_identifier,...)` al que se le pasa un id de un dataset a empujar a un CKAN y escribe los metadatos de ese dataset en el CKAN.

*Notas*

En la primera versión de esta función, la idea es escribir todos los metadatos tal cual están (mirror) en el nuevo CKAN. Más adelante futuras iteraciones van a introducir operaciones de transformación sobre los metadatos antes de empujarlos a un nuevo CKAN.

El módulo va a requerir pensar una manera transparente y fácil (o más de una) de manejar las credenciales necesarias para que pydatajson escriba en un CKAN.

Los campos de metadatos del perfil que no son parte original de CKAN, van a requerir discusiones para ver cómo aprovechar la API de CKAN y qué desarrollos hacen falta para interpretar esa metadata desde un CKAN Andino o datos.gob.ar, quienes van a poder usar muchos más campos de metadatos que un CKAN común.

I will be modeling search off of the search I created for vets.gov.  Since search for video can be complex I will take the following approach, in stages:

##Stage 0 - setting up search - Due August 4th

In this stage I will translate i14y to python, found here: https://github.com/GSA/i14y and set it up on AWS so that we can use elastic search as our search backend.

##Stage 1 - Naive search - due August 5th

In this version of search I will search based on known text - specifically search will be by title as well as by the person giving the talk

##Stage 2 - Labeled search added - due August 15th

In this version of search  I will search based on known text, and hand labeling that I will add to each video.

##Stage 3 - Classification for labeled search - due August 29th

In this version of search I will write scripts that classify videos into high level labels based on video title and presenter

##Stage 4 - Audio to text - due September 30th

In this version of search I will translate the video audio into text, allowing me to pull out summaries of the videos to appear below the videos as well as adding them to our search.  

##Stage 5 - Adding topic models allowing for classification by topic due October 30th

In this version I will add topic modeling to the audio of the video so that users can search by topic instead of by user or keyword alone.

##Stage 6 - Improving Search

Here I will begin adding tests to semantically check that search works intuitively for users.  Here will be more of an exploratory phase - looking into various summarization methods as well as other ways of efficiently doing search.  The i14y code base will be revisited and further changes will be made to ensure that search runs well.

Now that ``cmd2`` no longer needs to support Python 2.7 from release 0.9.0 onward, we can begin to add [type hinting](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html) to function/method arguments and return values.

Everyone is encouraged to begin doing this for all new and modified code.  In the long run it should make the code easier to read and maintain.

Note: If you use type hinting for function/method arguments and/or return values, then **just** use type hinting.  Don't put the type in a docstring comment if you are using type hinting.  With tools like the [mypy](http://mypy-lang.org) static analysis type checker and [PyCharm](https://www.jetbrains.com/help/pycharm/type-hinting-in-product.html), it is much easier to ensure that Python 3 optional type hints stay in sync with actual code changes.  It is much more likely that the documentation comments could get out of sync.  

*If you modify an existing function/method to add type hinting and it previously had type info in the docstring, then please remove the type info from the docstring.*

Please do NOT start adding type hinting to local variables as support for this syntax wasn't added until Python 3.6.  

Also, for now please be careful to use types from the ``typing`` module which exist in Python 3.5 and the ``typing`` module backport for Python 3.4 - a lot of additional types were added to that module in Python 3.6.

**TomekLinks** and **EditedNearestNeighbours** only remove samples form the majority class. However both methods are often used rather for data cleaning (removing samples form both classes) but undersampling (only removing samples form the majority class). Thus **SMOTETomek** and **SMOTEENN** are not implemented as proposed by [Batista, Prati and Monard (2004)](http://sci2s.ugr.es/keel/dataset/includes/catImbFiles/2004-Batista-SIGKDD.pdf), because they use TomekLinks and ENN for removing samples from the majority and the minority class.

It would be great to have a parameter that lets you choose whether to remove samples from both classes or only from the majority class.

```c++
    template <typename Type> array(size_t size, const Type *ptr, bool copy = true) {

            tmp = object(api.PyArray_NewCopy_(tmp.ptr(), -1 /* any order */), false);

```
I tried this and it seems to work just fine. Am I missing something here?

Thanks!

issue_title: Meta: Change governance model of pybind11 project?
issue_body:
Dear all,

here is a meta-topic that I'd like to discuss with the rest of the pybind11 community.

It's only been about ~1.5 years since the first code drop of this project landed on github, and many things have happened since then. The first is that this project has developed quite a significant following! We have a > 2K clones/day on good days, close to 2K "stars" (whatever that means ;)) and a significant amount of activity on the tracker and chat. It is clear that this project is doing quite well as far such metrics are concerned. 

The more important thing that these metrics don't capture is that we've also assembled a community of contributors who deeply care about this project, and who have fundamentally shaped the evolution of this project with improvements of absolutely outstanding quality. I'm constantly amazed how this has allowed pybind11 to scale to an ever-increasing number of needs while keeping its internal design simple and clean. Well done! :)

Thus far, I've acted as the BDFL and had the final say on a number of decisions. But being a busy tenure track prof, my availabilities for code reviews and and other open source-related activities are often unpredictable and far apart, hence this process has not always been smooth. It often seems to me as though I'm the bottleneck on things that should just get decided/merged right away. As the project grows further, this could eventually become problematic. But this way of organizing things also strikes me as silly: why should I be the one to make these decisions? Others are (demonstrably!) at least as qualified and invested into this project, hence I don't see why there needs to be a BDFL at all.

That motivates this meta-issue. I'm wondering if we should extend the leadership of this project or adopt a different kind of governance model. That makes it sound very bureaucratic, which is of course not the intent:  I just think that the project is big enough so that the sole responsibility should not rest on one person. An extreme change would be to adopt a formalized process like [C4](https://rfc.zeromq.org/spec:22/C4/) ([explanation](https://hintjens.gitbooks.io/social-architecture/content/chapter4.html)). A lighter weight option would be to promote a number of community members to moderators and try to reach a certain quorum based on how intrusive/useful a new feature is deemed to be before merging it (but then any moderator could do that). These are just a few possibilities to start a discussion -- of course I'm mainly interested in your ideas.

Best,
Wenzel

issue_title: CMake interaction with C/C++ std flags
issue_body:
This started in the discussion for #1089. The core issue was that C++ flags mix into C compilation, because PyBind has it's own implementation of C++ flags, and it doesn't include a way to separate C code.

CMake 2.8 was released in 2009, long before C++11 (much less the other standards) were released. Modern CMake does have a way to select C++ versions, and PyBind can be in contention with them.

Method 1: CMake 3.1+: `CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD` (and `CMAKE_CXX_STANDARD_REQUIRED`, `CMAKE_CXX_EXTENSIONS`). The current implementation does include checks for these, and turns off custom flags.
Method 2: CMake 3.1+: Manually listing features with `target_compile_features`. This was supposed to be the "best" way to do things, but listing every feature in C++11 is tedious.
Method 3: CMake 3.8: Meta-compile features were added for C++ standards, finally allowing `target_compile_features(my_target PUBLIC cxx_std_11)` or similar.

The first method is checked and appropriately handled by pybind11. The final method is not well supported by pybind11. I believe that by leaving `PYBIND_CPP_STANDARD` on CMake 3.8+, PyBind should default to this method. This should allow overriding the standard (automatically handled by CMake if you request it on a pybind target), as well as keep the C++/C separate without non-portable generator expressions.

And we can avoid the awful CPP/CXX mixed name by phasing out `PYBIND_CPP_STANDARD` on new targets 😉.

I use valgrind to check memory leak according to [this post](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/3982036/how-can-i-use-valgrind-with-python-c-extensions) on stack overflow. I found definite lost of memory if py::array is returned. 

For example, I have the following binding code, 

```cpp
#include <pybind11/pybind11.h>
#include <pybind11/numpy.h>
#include <vector>

namespace py = pybind11;


py::array_t<int> func() {

  std::vector<int> z(3, 1);

  return py::array( py::buffer_info(
    z.data(),
    sizeof(int),
    py::format_descriptor<int>::format(),
    1,
    {3},
    {sizeof(int)})
  );
}


PYBIND11_MODULE(demo, m) {
  m.def("func", &func);
}
```

and `func` is called with the snippet:
```python
import demo
print demo.func()
```

Valgrind will have the following report:

==23964== 5 bytes in 1 blocks are definitely lost in loss record 6 of 765
==23964==    at 0x4C2E80F: operator new[](unsigned long) (in /usr/lib/valgrind/vgpreload_memcheck-amd64-linux.so)

==23964==    by 0x68107BC: pybind11::detail::_(char const*) (descr.h:158)
==23964==    by 0x681E32A: name<int, 0> (numpy.h:999)

==23964==    by 0x681E32A: cpp_function<pybind11::array_t<int>, pybind11::name, pybind11::scope, pybind11::sibling> (pybind11.h:58)
==23964==    by 0x681E32A: pybind11::module& pybind11::module::def<pybind11::array_t<int, 16> (*)()>(char const*, pybind11::array_t<int, 16> (*&&)()) (pybind11.h:803)
==23964==    by 0x680F960: pybind11_init_demo (demo.cpp:24)
==23964==    by 0x680F960: pybind11_init_wrapper (demo.cpp:23)
==23964==    by 0x680F960: initdemo (demo.cpp:23)
==23964==    by 0x4F4FA94: _PyImport_LoadDynamicModule (importdl.c:53)
==23964==    by 0x4F4E0D0: import_submodule (import.c:2725)

According to the Return value policies, `return_value_policy::automatic` will be used in this case. I've tried `copy` and `move` explicitly, but none of them helps. Could this be a bug of pybind11?

Thanks!

issue_title: Cannot pass arbitrary object as py::object

issue_body:
In our project we have to perform some shutdown tasks. At the moment we use the Python API function `Py_AtExit` directly (Actually a pybind11 wrapper for this would be nice. Should I work on a MR for this?). I can attach a function that gets called when the python interpreter exits.

Now, when I tidy up remaining `py::object`s in the callback attached to `Py_AtExit`, the `handle::dec_ref` calls the Python API in the destructor of `py::object`. This is not allowed as stated in the CPython docs (https://docs.python.org/3.5/c-api/sys.html#c.Py_AtExit):

> Since Python’s internal finalization will have completed before the cleanup function, no Python APIs should be called by func.

Does anyone has a nice solution for this?

issue_title: Check if the channel_identifier that requested it is old before making an on-chain transaction
issue_body:
This is not in a direct connection with [raiden contracts 193](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden-contracts/issues/193) but stemmed out of the conversation from it as the easiest solution for #1907.

Essentially everytime we are about to make an on-chain call, check if the channel we are about to make the call for is the current channel and if not, do not perform the transaction.

For example in the case of #1907 it was due to checking the events since last restart and seeing there is a need to call `updateNonClosingBalanceProof()` since the counterparty closed while we were offline. But in the meantime the counterparty had reopened the channel and as such the `updateNonClosingBalanceProof()` is for a previous channel and will always fail.

To avoid that we need to add a check before [here](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/network/proxies/token_network.py#L586) for the `updateNonClosingBalanceProof()` case and make a call to the `TokenNetwork` proxy for the current channel identifier. If it's the same as the one that lead us to go down the path of making the on-chain transaction we will proceed. If not, we will just do nothing. Perhaps add a debug log saying the channel is outdated so we do nothing.

Do the same as above for all the blockchain transactions in [token_network.py](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/master/raiden/network/proxies/token_network.py)

I've being running my pipeline remotely but so far I've got way more failure than success, for not very obvious reasons. A disappointing fact is that it does not seem SoS is able to monitor job status well. Perhaps screenshot below can show what I'm experiencing:

![2017-05-15-13-30-29_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/917985/26072873/bea9f79c-3972-11e7-919d-905b7a71d586.png)

Terminal above is from my local computer and below is the remote computer. Apparently via `showq` command there is no job running under my account (in fact they all have failed). Yet `sos status` command still reports `submitted`/`completed`; therefore the screen above keeps showing the same info like a zombie. My `status_cmd` setting is `squeue --job {job_id}` and as seen from the screen shot when I run it directly there is an error message, so I'm a bit confused why the monitor does not quit on error. 

However locating the `.sh` in `~/.sos` and submit that file seems to work.

I understand it is very hard to eyeball the bug here. Using more verbosity may help but not always. I'm wondering if we can have a fall-back strategy, ie in this situation can SoS provide a way to give me all the relevant `.sh` files otherwise found in `~/.sos` and allow me to `qsub` each of them? I'm thinking some `dryrun` mode can provide this so that I can run these specific `sh` over and over again until all are executed, then finally run the original `sos` command to move on. This is a bit hacky but I think it is very difficult to ensure everything always works.

Right now the remote execution feature works like:

1. We are on a desktop (or server) with all the resources.
2. We write the script with the paths for this machine.
3. We define `path_map` etc so that local files could be synchronized to other servers.
4. We run the workflow mostly locally but farm some or all tasks to other servers. (e..g `sos run workflow -q cluster`)

This works well but the script depends on the local machine. I would recommend, thus document, the following way to make the workflow server neutral.

1. Define some directories that you think will be important in `config.yml`.
    ```
    localhost:
        project_dir:  /home/user/project
        resource_dir: /home/user/resource
    ```
    do this for all the servers that the script could be executed directly.

2. write your script as
    ```
    project_dir = CONFIG['localhost']['project_dir']
    resource_dir = 'CONFIG['localhost']['resource_dir']

    [10]
    depends: "${resource_dir}/hg19.fasta"
    ```

Now, if the `localhost` definition is made compatible with `path_map` of the server on other machines. The script would be

1. able to run on any machine without modification
2. able to run remotely on any machine without modification (with `path_map`)

Right now `group_by=int` requires that the number of samples is a multiple of `group_by`. I do not think this is a necessary restriction because for example if we have 45 files and would like to process 10 each time, `group_by=10` should handle it by `10, 10, 10, 10, 5`.

issue_title: Services from `/arlas/explore/*` accepting a filter in the `GET` or `POST` should also accept a filter in the `HEADER`
issue_body:
In gitlab by @sylvaingaudan on Aug 1, 2017, 11:57

Services from `/arlas/explore/*` accepting a filter in the `GET` or `POST` should also accept a filter in the `HEADER`.
The filter is specified in the JSON format and a `AND` operator is applied between the filter provided in the GET/POST and the filter provided in the `HEADER`.

Note: In some cases, the filter is currently appended as `OR`, if for example two `equal` operators are applied on a same field. This behavior is not acceptable for the present case : a real  `AND` operator must be applied.

issue_title: Default Stream quality to best? (documentation improvement)

### Description
I personally use streamlink for all my stream needs (mostly twitch), so I type a lot of streamlink commands into the terminal so any less typing would be appreciated. 

This would only require a minimal change (see [my fork](https://github.com/streamlink/streamlink/compare/master...Germandrummer92:master)) and would as far as I can see it not break anything, as even if someone just wanted to print the available qualities they are still printed and you can just exit the source stream immediately as you will most likely type into the same terminal window again.

If someone actually wants the behavior of just printing the stream qualities I could implement a separate option for that although I'm not sure anyone actually uses that.

### Expected / Actual behavior

`streamlink twitch.tv/pgl_dota`

defaults to
`streamlink twitch.tv/pgl_dota best` 
instead of just printing the stream qualities

Let me know if this makes sense and I would open a pull request with my one-line change!

### Description
Is it possible to have a plugin for girlfriendsfilmsvod.com (NSFW)?

To be perfectly honest, I have a limited understanding of how exactly this works, I'm still learning. Please forgive me if I use the incorrect terminology.

I've tried a few different ways of saving a copy of that stream, all of which failed. I even tried other VOD sites, to see if I could catch their streams, but they all offered the low-res download, and none had the HD stream. Short of screen capture _(shudders)_ you gentlemen/ladies are my last best hope.

I don't think DRM would have to be violated, but the plugin might have to support username and password authentication, or cookie sniffing. I think the streaming method being used is called Dash. The stream is avc mp4 video, with a bitrate of 4994kbps. Audio is mp4a.

issue_title: GAF Columns that process pipe separated IDs should drop entire line on any one bad ID
issue_body:
The GAF parser parses certain columns that are pipe separated IDs a little differently than other columns. For example, the reference column processes pipe separated PMID IDs: `PMID:12345|PMID:54321`. 

Currently, if any ID is found to be invalid (say because of bad characters in the ID) it is dropped, but the line remains. 

The bug is when there is one ID with an error, the ID is dropped, essentially making columns processed in this way potentially disappear. For example, if the reference column has:
`PMID:1234[2]` this gets converted by the parser into an empty list. 

We should drop the whole line if we see an error in any one pipe separated ID.

```python
    ({'foo': {'bar': [1, 2, 3]}}
        | should.be.a('dict')
        | should.have.key('foo')
        > should.have.key('bar'))
````

issue_title: Several issues with sizes and the `Image` widget
issue_body:
Though I came across these in working on #1677, this is separate and points out some problems in the current image widget independent of whether mouse/click traits are added.

I've tried to make this list fairly complete to create a checklist for a PR to fix; hopefully I can propose some solutions in the next day or two.

First, a bit of background: The `Image` widget accepts a width and height that are used to set the height/width tag on the `img` element in the front end. 

Also, this ended up a bit repetitive. There is [a notebook in a gist](https://gist.github.com/mwcraig/732e0cb75e695f29b9501b19b29795d9) working through all of this too.

1. The `Image` widget  width and height are, in all but the most simple case of an isolated image widget not in a `Box`, likely to be ignored, and will definitely be overridden by width/height properties of the containing box.  The reason for both is that CSS rules always override the width/height in the `img` tag.
1. The `height` is always ignored, even in an `Image` widget displayed by itself (no box) in a cell, because `height` is set by the notebook CSS to `auto`, so the height in image tag is ignored.
1.  The actual size of the image as displayed on screen is not exposed to python...those values are conceivably of interested for, e.g.,  preserving aspect ratio or scaling mouse clicks. The actual size should be exposed because it depends on things essentially unknowable to python, like the computed style that applies to the image once it is embedded in other Widgets (or a narrow browser window).
1. An image displayed in an isolated `Image` widget has its aspect ratio preserved.  The same widget, embedded in a `Box` whose `layout.height` (or width) is set will not be displayed with the intrinsic aspect ratio. The CSS wins, and the CSS doesn't know about aspect ratios...BUT...
1. ...the image display size can be set by setting `image_widget.layout.height` (or width), though...
1. ...you need to set both to guarantee the correct aspect ratio.
1. Putting an image in a box whose dimensions matches the image dimensions (i.e. the box layout's height and width match the height and width of the underlying image data) results in a display image that is 4 pixels smaller in height than the underlying image, though the display width is the underlying image width. This is because there is a margin of 2px in the CSS for `jupyter-widget` and the `height` is set to `auto`. No clue why the width isn't reduced if I set the `width` to `auto`.
1. Setting padding and/or a border on the `Image` widget reduces the displayed size of the image (which it should), but there is no way to get that size from python.
1. The JavaScript properties `width` and `height` of the `HTMLImageElement` for the image on the front end are always the rendered width/height of the image (which they are supposed to be), and are unrelated to with width/height in the image tag or, as a consequence, the width/height attributes in the back end.

issue_title: Is there an easier way to cancel groups of tasks?
issue_body:
I am struggling with a good way to manage groups of tasks.  The best I've come up with seems a bit convoluted involving a factory function.  Something along the lines of:
```
def cancellable_factory(func, cancel_scopes, shutdown_event=None, desc=""):
    async def scope_func():
        try:
            print(desc,"started")
            with trio.open_cancel_scope() as cancel_scope:
                cancel_scopes.append(cancel_scope)
                await func()
        except Exception as exc:
            print(desc, "crashed: {!r}".format(exc))
            if shutdown_event:
                shutdown_event.set()
        print(desc, "stopped")
    return scope_func

def wait_shutdown(cancel_scopes, shutdown_event):
    await shutdown_event.wait()
    for cancel_scope in cancel_scopes:
        cancel_scope.cancel()

cancel_scopes = []
shutdown_event = trio.Event()
nursery.spawn(cancellable_factory, blah_a, cancel_scopes, shutdown_event, "blahA")
nursery.spawn(cancellable_factory, blah_b, cancel_scopes, shutdown_event, "blahB")
nursery.spawn(cancellable_factory, blah_c, cancel_scopes, shutdown_event, "blahC")
nursery.spawn(wait_shutdown, cancel_scopes, shutdown_event)
```
Am I missing an easy way to manage groups of tasks (eg associated with each connection to a server)?

Is it possible to cancel a task directly instead of tracking separate cancel_scopes?  Something like:
```
taska = nursery.spawn(cancellable_factory, blah_a, shutdown_event, "blahA")
taskb = nursery.spawn(cancellable_factory, blah_b, shutdown_event, "blahB")
taskc = nursery.spawn(cancellable_factory, blah_c, shutdown_event, "blahC")
nursery.spawn(wait_shutdown, (taska, taskb, taskc), shutdown_event)
```

issue_body:
https://github.com/spulec/moto/issues/1588 addresses an issue where the Lambda Arn is not returned properly when returned in a DescribeStacksResponse. Once this fix is published upstream, the localstack moto version should be upgraded.

issue_title: It takes some time for a message to be invisible after receiving it
issue_body:
When you receive a message, it takes 2~5 seconds for it to be invisible, so, if you have multiple consumers, all of them will receive the same message.
Besides, if you're receiving a message from a Queue that is subscribed to a Topic with RawMessageDelivery set as true, the second time you get the message, the body will have the whole envelop and not just the body message.

Just an example:

The request:
http://localhost:4576/queue/my_queue?Action=ReceiveMessage

The first request

```xml
<ReceiveMessageResponse
	xmlns="http://queue.amazonaws.com/doc/2012-11-05/">
	<ReceiveMessageResult>
```

issue_title: [Help Needed] Empty response during lambda invocation
issue_body:
Firstly, thanks for this library!

When I upload a jar file to S3 and use that to invoke a lambda, I get a 200 response code, but with empty response.

```
    // creating S3 bucket to upload jar file
    val s3 = TestUtils.getClientS3
    val objectName = UUID.randomUUID().toString
    val bucketName = UUID.randomUUID.toString
    val functionName = UUID.randomUUID().toString

    // uploading jar file
    s3.createBucket(bucketName)
    s3.putObject(bucketName, objectName, new File("src/test/test.jar"))
    Assert.assertTrue(s3.doesObjectExist(bucketName, objectName))

    // creating function code with this S3 link
    val functionCode = new FunctionCode()
    functionCode.setS3Bucket(bucketName)
    functionCode.setS3Key(objectName)

    // creating a function request
    val request = new CreateFunctionRequest
    request.setFunctionName(functionName)
    request.setRuntime(Runtime.Java8)
    request.setCode(functionCode)
    request.setHandler(classOf[LambdaHandler].getName + "::handle")
    request.setTimeout(60)

    // creating lambda
    val lambda = TestUtils.getClientLambda
    lambda.createFunction(request)

    // invoking lambda
    val invokeRequest = new InvokeRequest()
      .withInvocationType(InvocationType.RequestResponse)
      .withFunctionName(functionName)
      .withPayload(UUID.randomUUID().toString)

    val invokeResult: InvokeResult = try {
      lambda.invoke(invokeRequest)
    } catch {
      case ex: AWSLambdaException => fail(ex)
    } finally {
      // cleaning up
      lambda.shutdown()
      s3.deleteObject(bucketName, objectName)
      s3.deleteBucket(bucketName)
    }
    println("result: " + StandardCharsets.UTF_8.decode(invokeResult.getPayload).toString)
    Assert.assertNull(invokeResult.getFunctionError)
    Assert.assertEquals(200, invokeResult.getStatusCode)
```

`result` in the above test is empty.
`jar` file has correct lambda code, because when I test it in AWS console, I get the right response.

Is there some configuration that I am missing?

Thanks in advance.
Should be converted to binary right away.

issue_title: gitlab.exceptions.GitlabError switched fields error_message and response_code

I recently updated to version 1.0.0 and was surprised by fact, that suddenly fields `error_message` and `response_code` of `gitlab.exceptions.GitlabError` are switched for no apparent reason. 

I found out, when I tried to use `gitlab.v4.objects.Project.branches.list(all = True)` on repository without access to it (403 followed). I didn't test, if this error happen anywhere else.

```python
user.identities # [] <--
```

```python
user.identities == [{'provider':'oauth2_generic', 'extern_uid':'3'}]
```

```python
user.identities == []
```

  - python-gitlab version: 1.4.0
  - API version you are using (v3/v4): v4
  - Gitlab server version (or gitlab.com): 10.4.2

We currently have a `WindowType` to represent the kind of duration to use for the window.  This limits how durations can be represented.

The [ISO 8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations) standard has representations for duration.  We can consider using this standard, which will reduce implementation complexity and allow greater expressiveness. 

This will likely require Window Aggregate to be sub-classed to Time Window and Count Window.

Is it possible to add the transformer after the creation of the Lark instance ?
And is it possible to combine multiple transform ?

Some code to make this sentence a bit clearer : 

Currently I do : 
```python
parser = Lark(grammar=mygrammar, parser='lalr', transformer=MyTransform())
```

I would like to do something along the lines of : 

```python
parser = Lark(grammar=mygrammar, parser='lalr')  # parses into AST
translater =  parser.transform(MyTransformer())  # transform AST into AST'
interpreter = translater.transform(MyInterpreter())  # transform AST' into python code
```

_OR_ I am going at this the wrong way and should do my transformation/interpretation of the AST outside of any Lark interface ? Is this at all in the scope of lark ?

issue_title: Literal.__str__ does not behave like unicode
issue_body:
pierre.a...@gmail.com, 2010-05-18T13:29:40.000Z 
Literal is a subclass of unicode.
However, its __str__ method has not the same semantics as unicode's,
because it uses the "unicode-escape" codec instead of the default system 
codec.

>>> from rdflib import Literal
>>> str(unicode('a\n'))
'a\n'
>>> str(Literal('a\n'))
'a\\n'
>>> str(unicode(Literal('a\n')))
'a\n'

This might be a feature, but breaks expectations: I tend to use Literals as 
any other unicode strings (slicing, startswith, etc...), so having to 
manage them specially only for str conversion seems a misfeature.

Comment 1 by eike...@gmail.com
Pierre, thank you for reporting this issue.

I know there are some unresolvable issues that arise from the choice of having Literal subclass unicode. I'm not 
sure if this is one of the unresolvable ones or not. We should take a look to see if we can fix this issue without 
breaking other bits. I'll work on a patch now to see what all fixing this will entail.


Comment 2 by eik...@gmail.com
This issue was closed by revision r1876.


Comment 3 by eike...@gmail.com
Unless this breaks bits for which we do not yet have a test case. We are good to go; the fix was fairly straight 
forward. If there are broken bits as a result of this change we'll need a test case for it.


Comment 4 by eik...@gmail.com
This issue was closed by revision a37a1cf8a191.
